- Default full CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim style id migration, style merge, or stylesheet semantic editing
  is implemented.
- Do not claim every non-worksheet package part has been made staged-only in
  this slice; P8.99 handles the broader staged-only removal.
- Do not expose staged chunks as public API in this slice.

## P8.99 - Source package-part materialized replacement removal

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor guard + broad test migration + regression + docs; no
public API or package format change.

Goal: remove the remaining old semantic where an existing non-workbook/docProps
source package part could still be replaced through `replace_part(std::string)`
as long as the payload stayed below the P8.92 size cap. This keeps source object
payloads on the staged chunk path and prevents media/drawing/chart/table/VBA/
calcChain/custom XML/comments/persons/OLE/control/root/unknown parts from
re-entering materialized replacement state.

Input:
- P8.40/P8.41 added `replace_part_chunks()` for generic non-worksheet source
  parts.
- P8.92 added a temporary size guard for materialized source part replacement.
- P8.97/P8.98 already made sharedStrings/styles staged-only.

Output:
- Removed `package_editor_materialized_source_part_replacement_byte_limit`.
- `PackageEditor::replace_part(std::string)` now rejects any existing source
  package part unless it is `/xl/workbook.xml`, core properties, or app
  properties, which keep their separate bounded small-XML guards.
- Object preservation and same-path ordering tests for root/unknown extension,
  calcChain, drawing/table/chart/VML, pivot/external links, custom XML,
  comments/threaded comments/persons, OLE/control, and background picture/VML now
  queue replacements through `replace_part_chunks()` memory chunks and assert
  `StreamRewrite` state.
- The generic source-part materialized replacement regression now verifies
  no-state-pollution for a small unknown extension payload, not only an oversized
  payload.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Default full CTest remains green.
- Regression proves rejected materialized source part replacement leaves
  EditPlan, manifest, planned output, and output bytes unmodified, while staged
  chunks still work.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim public low-memory editor API is complete.
- Do not claim workbook/core/app XML is streamed; those remain bounded small XML.
- Do not remove preservation/audit semantics for object parts; only the payload
  staging boundary changed.

## P8.100 - Reject staged small-XML package part replacements

Status: done.

Goal: remove the staged workbook/core/app small-XML half-state. These metadata
parts are intentionally bounded and materialized by `replace_part(std::string)`;
letting `replace_part_chunks()` queue them as `StreamRewrite` made later by-name
catalog/calc or metadata helpers fail only after a planned staged small-XML
part existed.

Input:
- P8.99 source package-part materialized replacement removal.
- Existing workbook staged-chunk by-name failure regression.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::replace_part_chunks()` now rejects `/xl/workbook.xml`,
  core properties, and app properties targets before state mutation.
- The old fullCalcOnLoad-specific staged workbook guard is removed because the
  generic small-XML staged guard catches these chunks earlier.
- The repeated workbook replacement regression now uses materialized
  `replace_part()` for both workbook states.
- The staged small-XML regression now verifies workbook/core/app entry-point
  rejection and no-state-pollution instead of preserving staged `StreamRewrite`
  half-states for metadata XML parts.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Default full CTest remains green.
- Regression proves rejected staged workbook/core/app small-XML chunks leave EditPlan,
  manifest, planned output, calc policy, and output bytes unmodified.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim workbook/core/app XML is low-memory streamed.
- Do not reintroduce staged small-XML chunks just to support metadata helpers;
  workbook catalog/calc metadata remains the bounded small-XML path.

## P8.101 - Broad materialized part output guard

Status: done.

Goal: remove the last worksheet-only save-time wording from active
`PackagePartReplacement::materialized_small_xml` materialization. If an impossible
internal state ever queues a materialized source package part outside the
workbook/core/app small-XML boundary, output assembly must reject it generically
instead of only checking worksheet parts.

Input:
- P8.99 source package-part materialized replacement removal.
- P8.100 staged small-XML rejection.

Output:
- Replaced the duplicated workbook/core/app allowlist helpers with
  `package_part_is_materialized_small_xml_boundary()`.
- `materialize_active_part_replacement()` now rejects any materialized
  source package part replacement unless that source part is workbook/core/app
  small XML. Generated small-XML replacements remain valid only for generated /
  non-source metadata states, not as a bypass for existing non-small source
  parts.
- Removed the stale worksheet-only materialized planned-output guard and its
  dead helper.
- Extended the staged small-XML no-state-pollution regression to cover source
  `docProps/core.xml` and `docProps/app.xml`, not just `/xl/workbook.xml`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Default full CTest remains green.
- Regression proves rejected staged workbook/core/app small-XML chunks preserve source
  bytes and planned output state.

Forbidden:
- Do not reopen materialized source replacements for sharedStrings, styles,
  media, drawings, charts, tables, VBA, comments, custom XML, OLE/control or
  unknown extension parts.
- Do not claim workbook/core/app XML is streamed; these remain bounded
  materialized small-XML paths.

## P8.102 - Planned-output materialized reason source guard alignment

Status: done.

Goal: keep `planned_output()` audit text aligned with the broad source-part
materialized output guard from P8.101. Even defensive / impossible internal
states should describe non-small source package part materialized replacements
as rejected staged-only states, not as generated small-XML payloads.

Input:
- P8.101 broad `materialize_active_part_replacement()` source-part guard.
- `PackageEditorOutputEntryPlan::materialized_replacement_reason`.

Output:
- `materialized_part_replacement_reason()` now resolves the source part first
  and applies `package_part_is_materialized_small_xml_boundary()` before
  reporting workbook/core/app or generated small-XML materialized payload
  reasons.
- The reason path now mirrors the save-time guard: any source package part
  outside workbook/core/app small XML is classified as a disallowed
  materialized source package part state before planned output.
- Test memory-chunk replacement helper naming now uses `chunk_payload` instead
  of `materialized_data`, so staged chunk tests do not imply a string
  replacement boundary.
- No public API is added and no materialized source replacement path is
  reopened.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Default full CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose a test-only mutator for corrupting `PackageEditor` internals
  only to hit an impossible snapshot reason branch.
- Do not treat generated small XML as a bypass for existing non-small source
  parts.

## P8.103 - Save-time source metadata copy becomes file-backed

Status: done.

Goal: remove the last `PackageEditor::save_as()` source-copy `read_entry()`
fallback. Copy-original metadata entries should behave like other source entries:
they are extracted to a temporary file and handed to `PackageWriter` as
file-backed chunks, not materialized strings.

Input:
- P8.79/P8.82 historical metadata-only source-copy materialization boundary.
- P8.101/P8.102 broad materialized source-part guard and output-plan reason cleanup.

Output:
- Removed `PackageEditorOutputEntryPlan::materialized_source_metadata_copy` and
  `materialized_source_metadata_copy_reason`.
- Removed `read_materialized_source_metadata_copy()` from `src/package_editor.cpp`.
- `source_entry_file_backed_copy_reason()` now marks copy-original source package
  parts, `[Content_Types].xml`, package `_rels/.rels`, source-owned `.rels`, and
  defensive generic metadata entries as file-backed source copy.
- `materialize_planned_output_entry()` now requires `file_backed_source_copy` for
  every source-copy entry and always uses `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()`
  plus `PackageEntryChunk::file()`.
- Package-editor tests now assert metadata `.rels` source-copy entries are
  file-backed rather than materialized.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Default full CTest remains green.
- Production `src/package_editor.cpp` direct `reader.read_entry()` use is limited
  to named workbook small-XML helper reads; source-copy output assembly no longer
  has a `read_entry()` fallback.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim PackageReader metadata ingestion is streamed; it still uses bounded
  small-XML materialization.
- Do not claim workbook/core/app active metadata replacements are streamed; they
  remain bounded small-XML materialized payloads.
- Do not expose a public low-memory editor API in this slice.

## P8.104 - Helper-managed package parts leave entry-level materialized state

Status: done.

Goal: finish the next C5 naming/ownership cleanup: real package parts must not be
represented as materialized package-entry replacements. Keep entry-level
materialized state exclusively for OPC metadata entries.

Input:
- P8.89/P8.90 narrowed package-entry materialized replacements but still allowed
  workbook/core/app helper output to live in `PackageEntryReplacement`.
- P8.103 removed save-time source-copy `read_entry()` fallback, leaving active
  helper-managed payload classification as the next cleanup point.

Output:
- `upsert_entry_replacement()` now accepts only metadata entry names:
  `[Content_Types].xml`, package `_rels/.rels`, and source-owned `.rels`.
- Workbook calc metadata, workbook sheet-catalog rename, and core/app docProps
  helpers now queue their materialized small XML through `PackagePartReplacement`.
- `current_planned_materialized_workbook_xml()` only reads planned workbook XML
  from `PackagePartReplacement` or the guarded source workbook helper; it no
  longer checks an entry-level workbook replacement fallback.
- `planned_output()` and save-time materialization prefer package-part
  replacements before package-entry replacements, so stale entry state cannot
  override an active real package part.
- `PackageEntryReplacement::materialized_data` is documented as active metadata
  only, and docProps output-plan tests assert generated core/app payloads are
  materialized package-part replacements.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Default full CTest remains green.
- Output-plan reasons distinguish generated small-XML package parts from
  metadata package-entry replacements.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim workbook/core/app helper payloads are streamed; they remain
  bounded small-XML materialized package-part payloads.
- Do not use `PackageEntryReplacement` as a compatibility shim for any package
  part, including workbook and docProps.
- Do not expose a public low-memory editor API in this slice.

## P8.105 - Workbook planned XML helpers stop accepting package-entry state

Status: done.

Goal: remove a stale compatibility-shaped parameter from the C5 workbook
planned-input helpers. The code already no longer reads workbook/core/app payloads
from `PackageEntryReplacement`; the function signatures now enforce the same
boundary.

Input:
- P8.104 moved helper-managed `/xl/workbook.xml` and core/app docProps payloads to
  `PackagePartReplacement::materialized_small_xml`.
- `current_planned_materialized_workbook_xml()` and the by-name worksheet resolver
  still accepted `entry_replacements`, even though workbook XML was no longer read
  from that state.

Output:
- `current_planned_materialized_workbook_xml()` now takes only the `PackageReader`,
  package-part replacements, and a purpose string.
- `resolve_worksheet_part_by_name_for_patch()` now takes only the reader, manifest,
  package-part replacements, and sheet name.
- By-name worksheet helpers still preserve explicit workbook planned removal
  semantics through the manifest, but they no longer present metadata entry
  replacement state as a possible workbook XML source.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green after the signature cleanup.
- No call site can pass `entry_replacements_` into workbook planned XML resolution.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim workbook XML is streamed; it remains bounded small XML when planned
  or read from source.
- Do not remove metadata entry replacements; `[Content_Types].xml`, package
  `_rels/.rels`, and source-owned `.rels` still use that internal path.
- Do not expose a public low-memory editor API in this slice.

## P8.106 - Event-reader window naming drops full worksheet implication

Status: done.

Goal: remove a misleading internal name left after the worksheet event reader was
converted to pull-based chunk sources. The helper consumes the current bounded
window, not a complete worksheet XML buffer.

Input:
- P8.72 removed the event-reader / transformer single-buffer and chunk-vector
  convenience APIs.
- The private `consume_available_events()` helper still named its input
  `worksheet_xml`, which could be mistaken for a full worksheet materialization.

Output:
- `consume_available_events()` now takes `std::string_view xml_window`.
- Production event-reader / transformer sources no longer contain a
  `worksheet_xml` variable name in the chunk-source path.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green, proving worksheet rewrite callers still
  work through the event-reader chunk-source path.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim this changes memory behavior by itself; it is a naming/state
  hygiene cleanup on top of the existing chunk-source path.
- Do not expose a public low-memory editor API in this slice.

## P8.107 - Package-part materialized payload is small-XML named

Status: done.

Goal: finish the current C5 naming boundary cleanup for active package-part
payloads. After helper-managed workbook/core/app parts left metadata
package-entry state, the remaining package-part materialized payload must not
look like an arbitrary part or worksheet string.

Input:
- P8.104 moved helper-managed workbook/core/app package parts from
  `PackageEntryReplacement` to `PackagePartReplacement`.
- The package-part field and call sites still used generic materialized-payload
  wording in places, which made bounded small XML look like a broad string
  replacement path.

Output:
- `PackagePartReplacement::materialized_small_xml` is the only package-part
  string payload field.
- `upsert_part_replacement()` and `PackageEditor::replace_part()` name their
  package-part string parameter `materialized_small_xml`.
- Save-time materialization and workbook planned XML helpers read
  `materialized_small_xml` only through the workbook/core/app or generated
  small-XML boundary.
- `PackageEntryReplacement::materialized_data` remains metadata-entry-only for
  `[Content_Types].xml`, package `_rels/.rels`, and source-owned `.rels`.

Acceptance:
- Source audit has no `PackagePartReplacement::materialized_data` references.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim workbook/core/app helper payloads are streamed; they remain
  bounded small-XML materialized payloads.
- Do not rename or remove metadata-entry `PackageEntryReplacement::materialized_data`.
- Do not expose a public low-memory editor API in this slice.

## P8.108 - Stored read_entry materialization reuses chunk-source adapter

Status: done.

Goal: remove the last direct full-read branch inside `PackageReader::read_entry()`.
The API still returns a materialized `std::string`, but the implementation should
not keep a separate stored-entry path that bypasses the same chunk-source reader
used by worksheet scanning and DEFLATE materialization.

Input:
- P8.28 added direct stored ZIP-entry chunk sources.
- P8.32 made the minizip DEFLATE `read_entry()` branch consume
  `entry_chunk_source()` before returning a materialized string.
- Stored/no-compression `read_entry()` still used a direct `read_bytes_at()`
  branch.

Output:
- `PackageReader::read_entry()` now always materializes by consuming
  `entry_chunk_source()` for supported entry methods.
- Stored entries therefore use the same incremental chunk reader and CRC check
  path as the rest of the reader-side chunk-source pipeline.
- Unsupported compression behavior remains delegated to `entry_chunk_source()`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Documentation states that `read_entry()` still materializes because it returns
  `std::string`; this is implementation unification, not a public low-memory API.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim `read_entry()` is low-memory; it still returns a complete string.
- Do not remove `read_entry()` tests that intentionally verify materialized
  public/internal readback behavior.
- Do not expose a public streaming reader API in this slice.

## P8.109 - Bound materialized replacement cell XML payload

Status: done.

Goal: put an explicit size guard on the remaining caller-materialized single-cell
replacement edge. `replace_worksheet_cells()` no longer keeps full worksheet
input/output strings, but each replacement `<c>` fragment is still provided by
the caller as a complete XML view; that boundary must be named and bounded.

Input:
- P8.83 renamed the single-cell payload field to
  `materialized_replacement_cell_xml`.
- Worksheet transformer and PackageEditor cell replacement paths both preflight
  replacement payloads before mutating Patch state.

Output:
- Added internal
  `worksheet_replacement_cell_xml_materialization_byte_limit` in the worksheet
  transformer layer.
- `build_replacement_index()` rejects oversized replacement cell XML before
  parsing the replacement or scanning source worksheet chunks.
- Transformer and PackageEditor regressions prove oversized replacement cell XML
  fails before state changes while normal chunk-source worksheet rewrite paths
  remain unchanged.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim replacement cell XML is streamed; this slice bounds the remaining
  single-cell materialized payload instead of removing it.
- Do not expose a public replacement-cell streaming API in this slice.
- Do not reintroduce worksheet-level materialized planned input.

## P8.110 - Preflight generic staged package-entry chunks before Patch state

Status: done.

Goal: keep the staged-chunk replacement path from accepting invalid chunk
descriptors into `PackageEditor` planned state. Generic non-worksheet
`replace_part_chunks()` should fail before mutating `EditPlan` / manifest state
when a chunk mixes memory and file sources, points at a missing file, overflows
the measured size, or uses an unsupported chunk kind.

Input:
- P8.41 made generic non-worksheet staged replacements commit chunks directly
  instead of first creating an empty materialized replacement placeholder.
- P9.5 already made `PackageWriter` reject invalid chunked `PackageEntry`
  payloads before opening output, but generic `PackageEditor::replace_part_chunks()`
  could still queue invalid non-worksheet chunks and defer failure to `save_as()`.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::replace_part_chunks()` now runs the existing chunk-size/source
  preflight for generic non-worksheet staged replacements after target and
  small-XML guard checks, but before `upsert_part_replacement_chunks()`,
  manifest write-mode mutation, or `EditPlan` mutation.
- Added a `fastxlsx.package_editor` regression for mixed memory/file and missing
  file generic chunks. Both failures preserve edit-plan entries/notes, package
  entry audits, manifest write modes, calc policy, planned-output staged flags,
  and later safe `save_as()` output bytes.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not move workbook/core/app small XML onto staged chunks; those remain on
  the bounded materialized metadata path.
- Do not claim this is a public streaming package writer API or full package
  low-memory pipeline.
- Do not change worksheet staged replacement validation/audit semantics in this
  slice.

## P8.111 - Preflight explicit worksheet staged chunk descriptors before scan

Status: done.

Goal: make explicit worksheet staged replacements reject invalid
`PackageEntryChunk` descriptors at the same boundary as generic staged
replacements, before root validation opens chunk readers or any worksheet
rewrite state can be queued.

Input:
- P8.35 made `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` validate worksheet roots,
  payload dependency audit, and relationship-id audit from real staged chunks.
- P8.110 added descriptor preflight for generic non-worksheet staged chunks,
  but explicit worksheet staged chunks still relied on the validation reader to
  discover mixed memory/file descriptors or missing file chunks.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` now runs
  `require_staged_package_entry_chunks_valid()` after worksheet target /
  workbook / calc-policy preconditions and before constructing any
  `PackageEntryChunkReader`.
- Mixed memory/file worksheet chunks and missing file worksheet chunks now fail
  with `worksheet staged replacement has invalid staged chunks: ...`.
- Added a `fastxlsx.package_editor` no-state-pollution regression that checks
  edit-plan entries/notes, package-entry audits, relationship/payload audits,
  manifest write modes, calc policy, planned-output staged/materialized flags,
  and later safe `save_as()` output bytes.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken worksheet XML root validation, payload dependency audit,
  relationship-id audit, or calc metadata handling.
- Do not turn worksheet staged chunks into a public low-memory editor API.
- Do not claim replacement cell XML is streamed; that remains a separately
  bounded single-cell materialized payload.

## P8.112 - Preflight prevalidated worksheet staged chunk commits

Status: done.

Goal: close the remaining internal staged-chunk commit boundary so future
worksheet output-side handoffs cannot queue invalid `PackageEntryChunk`
descriptors after validation/audit data has already been precomputed.

Input:
- P8.111 added descriptor preflight to the explicit
  `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` entry point, before root validation and
  audit readers are constructed.
- `replace_worksheet_part_prevalidated_chunks()` is also used by bounded
  `sheetData` replacement and cell replacement output-side paths after they
  have written PackageEditor-owned temp-file chunks and precomputed audits.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_part_prevalidated_chunks()` now runs
  `require_staged_package_entry_chunks_valid()` after worksheet target /
  workbook / calc-policy preconditions and before payload policy checks,
  workbook calc metadata rewrites, manifest mutation, `EditPlan` mutation, or
  replacement chunk commit.
- Explicit staged worksheet replacements still fail at the earlier P8.111
  boundary; this slice adds a private defense-in-depth guard for prevalidated
  internal handoffs and future callers.
- Existing `fastxlsx.package_editor` staged worksheet, sheetData output, and
  cell replacement output regressions exercise the successful prevalidated
  commit path; P8.111 continues to cover invalid descriptor no-state-pollution
  at the callable staged worksheet boundary.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not reintroduce materialized worksheet replacement placeholders.
- Do not weaken precomputed worksheet payload audit, relationship-id audit, or
  calc metadata handling.
- Do not claim this is a public low-memory worksheet editor API.

## P8.113 - Contextualize caller chunk-source staging failures

Status: done.

Goal: make caller-provided worksheet / sheetData chunk-source failures explicit
at the staging boundary and keep the low-memory staged path's no-state-pollution
contract visible.

Input:
- `replace_worksheet_part_from_chunk_source()` writes caller worksheet chunks to
  a scoped temp file before reusing staged chunk validation/audit/calc handling.
- `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` writes caller replacement
  `<sheetData>` chunks to a bounded scoped temp file before local worksheet
  rewrite and final staged worksheet commit.
- Both paths already avoided committing state before staging succeeded, but a
  throwing caller callback surfaced without naming which staging boundary was
  being read.

Output:
- `write_complete_file_from_chunk_source()` now wraps only the caller
  `read_next_chunk()` invocation with `failed while reading <context>: ...`.
  the then-current sheetData replacement staging helper applied the same read
  failure wrapping while preserving the existing size/write errors as their own
  messages; P8.127 later replaced that helper with direct caller chunk
  consumption during rewritten worksheet output.
- Added `fastxlsx.package_editor` regressions for throwing complete worksheet
  and sheetData chunk sources. They verify contextual errors, stopped reads,
  unchanged `EditPlan` / notes / calc policy / manifest write mode, and no new
  PackageEditor temp files.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not catch and relabel bounded-size failures or file-write failures as
  caller read failures.
- Do not transfer scoped staging temp files to editor-owned cleanup until the
  prevalidated worksheet commit succeeds.
- Do not claim caller-provided complete worksheet XML is a public low-memory API.

## P8.114 - Use non-owning selector views in worksheet cell replacement index

Status: done.

Goal: keep the remaining single-cell replacement boundary from copying selector
strings into the transformer index. Replacement cell XML remains caller-owned
and bounded; selectors should also stay caller-owned while the synchronous
transform runs.

Input:
- P8.109 bounded each caller-materialized replacement `<c>` payload.
- `WorksheetTransformAction` already carries non-owning views, and
  PackageEditor cell replacement analysis/output does not retain an action plan.
- The transformer still copied every replacement selector into a
  `std::map<std::string, std::string_view>` and copied matched selectors into a
  `std::set<std::string>`.

Output:
- `src/worksheet_transformer.cpp` now uses
  `std::map<std::string_view, std::string_view>` for the replacement index and
  `std::set<std::string_view>` for matched selectors.
- Missing replacement diagnostics still materialize owned `std::string`
  entries in `WorksheetTransformSummary::missing_cell_references`, so caller
  selector views do not leak through the summary.
- `WorksheetCellReplacement` documentation now states that both views must
  remain alive and stable for the duration of the transformer call.
- Added a `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` regression that mutates caller
  selector/payload storage after the call and verifies the missing diagnostic
  remains owned.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not make `WorksheetTransformSummary` hold string_views.
- Do not remove the single-cell replacement XML byte limit.
- Do not claim cell replacement payload XML is streamed; it remains a bounded
  caller-materialized single-cell payload.

## P8.115 - Trust prevalidated cell replacement payloads on follow-up passes

Status: superseded by P8.125/P8.126; transitional API removed.

Goal: avoid reparsing each caller-materialized replacement `<c>` payload during
the same `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells()` call after the initial
analysis pass has already validated the payload root/reference/size.

Input:
- P8.114 made the transformer replacement index non-owning for selectors and
  payloads.
- At the time of P8.115, `replace_worksheet_cells()` still ran several source
  worksheet passes: analysis/dimension/payload audit, relationship-id audit, and
  output writing. P8.117 later fused relationship-id audit into output writing.
- Before this slice, relationship-id audit and output writing rebuilt the
  transformer index and repeated replacement `<c>` payload validation.

Historical output:
- This slice originally added an internal validation-mode switch so follow-up
  passes could reuse already checked replacement payload views.
- P8.125 replaced that mechanism with an explicit
  `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan`.
- P8.126 deleted the validation-mode enum and trust-mode path entirely. Current
  code has no `TrustPrevalidated` bypass; every replacement set must be
  preflighted while building the plan.

Current acceptance:
- Source/header/test references to the transitional validation-mode symbols stay
  removed.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose this as public editor API.
- Do not skip the first analysis pass validation.
- Do not remove the bounded single-cell replacement XML materialization limit.
- Do not claim replacement cell XML is streamed; this only removes repeated
  validation of the already materialized single-cell payload.

## P8.116 - Move cell replacement audit vectors into prevalidated commit

Status: done.

Goal: avoid retaining duplicate copies of already-built cell-replacement audit
notes/audit records at the final output-side worksheet commit boundary.

Input:
- `replace_worksheet_cells()` already stages dimension-refreshed worksheet output
  as a PackageEditor-owned file-backed chunk.
- The analysis pass builds `WorksheetPayloadDependencyAuditResult`; after
  P8.117 the output pass builds `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceAuditResult`
  while writing emitted chunks.
- Other worksheet staged and sheetData paths already move audit vectors into
  `replace_worksheet_part_prevalidated_chunks()`.

Output:
- `replace_worksheet_cells()` now moves payload audit notes/audit records,
  relationship-reference notes/audit records, and the replacement reason into
  `replace_worksheet_part_prevalidated_chunks()`.
- This removes one temporary duplicate of audit strings/records after output
  staging while preserving the same `EditPlan` / planned-output audit visibility.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken payload-policy or relationship-policy preflight.
- Do not remove structured audit propagation from the final `EditPlan`.
- Do not claim this changes the remaining bounded caller-materialized
  replacement `<c>` payload boundary.

## P8.117 - Fuse cell replacement relationship audit into output pass

Status: done.

Goal: remove one more planned/current worksheet rescan from
`PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells()`. The initial analysis pass still owns
dimension refresh, missing selector detection, and first replacement-payload
validation; the output pass now also scans the emitted XML chunks for worksheet
relationship references.

Input:
- P8.115 made follow-up cell replacement passes trust the already-validated
  replacement `<c>` payloads.
- P8.116 moved generated audit vectors into the final prevalidated commit.
- Before this slice, cell replacement still scanned the current worksheet three
  times: analysis, relationship-id audit, and output writing.

Output:
- Removed the cell-replacement-only relationship audit pass over the current
  worksheet input.
- `write_worksheet_cell_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()` now writes the
  dimension-refreshed file-backed output and feeds the same emitted chunks into
  `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner`.
- `replace_worksheet_cells()` now preserves the relationship-policy-before-payload
  policy order by staging output first, collecting relationship audit from the
  emitted chunks, and then rejecting relationship policy followed by payload
  policy before any `EditPlan`, manifest, package-entry, calc, or replacement
  state mutation.
- Added a `fastxlsx.package_editor` regression for relationship-policy failure
  and payload-policy failure after output staging, including scoped
  temporary-file cleanup and no-state pollution.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Cell replacement source/planned worksheet scans are reduced from three
  transformer scans to two: analysis and output-with-relationship-audit.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken relationship audit propagation.
- Do not turn relationship audit into repair/pruning.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion; this is still an internal
  C5 pass-count and materialization-boundary cleanup.

## P8.118 - Fuse staged worksheet chunk payload and relationship audits

Status: done.

Goal: remove one staged worksheet replacement audit reread from
`PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` while preserving the same
root-validation, dependency-audit, relationship-audit, calc metadata, and
no-state-pollution semantics.

Input:
- P8.35 made explicit staged worksheet replacements validate root XML, payload
  dependencies, and relationship ids from real `PackageEntryChunk` inputs.
- Before this slice, the staged worksheet helper read those chunks three times:
  root validation, payload dependency audit, then relationship-id audit.
- Relationship-id scanning still needs event-reader namespace context, while
  payload dependency scanning only needs start tags.

Output:
- Added `WorksheetReplacementPayloadAuditCollector` for the combined audit
  scanner and removed the now-unused payload-only full-worksheet chunk-source
  scanner from `package_editor.cpp`.
- `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` now uses one event-reader chunk-source pass
  to validate worksheet root/events and collect payload dependency audit plus
  worksheet relationship-id audit over the staged chunks.
- The combined audit pass feeds each emitted start tag into the payload
  collector and the same emitted XML chunks into `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner`,
  preserving namespace-aware relationship-id audit without materializing the
  worksheet payload.
- Existing staged worksheet, generic worksheet chunk routing, by-name staged
  chunk, and chunk-source worksheet replacement tests now assert the combined
  audit reader note.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Explicit staged worksheet replacement chunk reads are reduced from three
  passes to one event-reader validation/audit pass.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken worksheet XML root validation, relationship-id audit propagation,
  payload dependency audit propagation, or calc metadata handling.
- Do not turn relationship audit into repair/pruning.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion; complete worksheet
  replacement caller input is still staged before commit, and workbook/small-XML
  materialization boundaries remain.

## P8.119 - Fuse sheetData rewrite output relationship audit into output writer

Status: done.

Goal: remove the post-output worksheet reread from bounded
`replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` after it has already generated
the rewritten worksheet XML as a PackageEditor-owned file-backed staged chunk.

Input:
- The sheetData local rewrite path already writes rewritten worksheet XML through
  `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()`.
- Before this slice, it then reopened the staged worksheet chunk through a
  `PackageEntryChunkReader` only to collect worksheet relationship-id audit.
- Cell replacement P8.117 already proved relationship-id audit can be collected
  from emitted output chunks while writing a staged worksheet temp file.

Output:
- `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()` now accepts
  an optional `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner` and feeds both preserved
  source worksheet chunks and inserted replacement `<sheetData>` chunks into it
  while writing the output file.
- `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` now builds
  `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceAuditResult` directly from that output writer
  scanner, or emits the existing parse-failure audit note if relationship
  scanning fails.
- The old post-output `PackageEntryChunkReader` relationship-only reread was
  removed from the sheetData path.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now checks the sheetData output-plan note for the
  fused relationship-id audit handoff.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- SheetData local rewrite output-side worksheet scans are reduced by one:
  relationship-id audit is collected during output writing instead of after
  output staging.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken sheetData root validation, replacement payload audit,
  preservation audit, relationship-id audit propagation, or calc metadata
  handling.
- Do not turn sheetData local rewrite into a true large-file transformer; it
  remains bounded and still stages the rewritten worksheet output.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion.

## P8.120 - Fuse sheetData preservation audit into output writer

Status: done.

Goal: remove the separate current-worksheet preservation-audit scan from
bounded sheetData local rewrite, so current worksheet input is no longer read
once only to collect preserved metadata notes before the output rewrite reads it
again.

- Before this slice, `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` scanned
  the current worksheet input through `worksheet_sheet_data_preservation_audit_from_chunk_source()`,
  then reopened the same source/planned staged chunks for
  `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()`.
- P8.119 already taught the output writer to collect relationship-id audit while
  emitting rewritten chunks.

Output:
- Added `WorksheetSheetDataPreservationAuditCollector`, which records the same
  structured preserved worksheet metadata audits from worksheet events.
- `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()` now accepts
  the preservation collector and feeds it the current worksheet events while the
  output rewrite consumes them.
- `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` now merges replacement
  `<sheetData>` payload audit into the preservation audit produced by the output
  writer.
- Removed the now-unused `worksheet_sheet_data_preservation_audit_from_chunk_source()`
  preservation-only scanner.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now checks the sheetData output-plan note for the
  fused preservation-audit handoff.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- SheetData local rewrite current worksheet input scans are reduced by one:
  preservation metadata audit is collected during output writing instead of a
  separate pre-output pass.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken replacement `<sheetData>` validation or replacement payload
  policy preflight; replacement payload audit still runs before output staging.
- Do not weaken preserved metadata audit propagation, relationship-id audit
  propagation, calc metadata handling, or no-state-pollution behavior.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion.

## P8.121 - Fuse sheetData replacement payload validation and audit

Status: done.

Goal: remove the duplicate read of staged replacement `<sheetData>` chunks in
`replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()`. At the time of this slice,
  replacement payload root validation, bounded payload-size guarding, staged-file
  write, and replacement payload dependency audit became one caller chunk-source
  pass before output staging and before any Patch state mutation; P8.127 later
  removed the separate staged replacement sheetData file entirely.

Input:
- `worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_audit_from_chunk_source()` already used
  `scan_sheet_data_start_tags_from_chunk_source()`, which validates the
  replacement `<sheetData>` root, single-root structure, comments, closing tags,
  and bounded parser window while emitting start tags for audit.
- The caller still ran a separate `validate_sheet_data_replacement_xml_from_chunk_source()`
  pass before reopening the same staged chunks for replacement payload audit.

Output:
- Removed the unused `validate_sheet_data_replacement_xml_from_chunk_source()`
  helper.
- `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` now reads replacement
  `<sheetData>` chunks once for combined root/structure validation, bounded
  payload-size guarding, and replacement payload dependency audit. Current code
  does that direct read during rewritten worksheet output insertion rather than
  through a separate replacement staging file.
- The existing payload policy rejection still runs after the combined
  validation/audit pass and before Patch state mutation; in the current direct
  insertion implementation this can occur after the scoped rewritten worksheet
  temp file has been written, but before it is committed to editor-owned state.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now checks the output-plan note for the combined
  replacement payload validation/audit handoff.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Replacement `<sheetData>` caller chunk reads in the sheetData local rewrite
  path are reduced: validation, bounded size guarding, payload dependency audit,
  and current output insertion share one caller chunk-source pass.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken replacement `<sheetData>` XML root validation, malformed XML
  rejection, bounded payload-size guard, or payload-policy no-state-pollution.
- Do not move replacement payload audit after Patch state mutation.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion.

## P8.122 - Fuse full worksheet staged replacement validation and audit

Status: done.

Goal: remove the remaining duplicate read of staged complete worksheet
replacement chunks in `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_part_chunks()`.
The same event-reader chunk-source pass should validate worksheet root/events,
collect replacement payload dependency audit, and collect worksheet
relationship-id references before any Patch state mutation.

Input:
- P8.118 had already fused payload dependency audit and relationship-id audit,
  but still ran a separate root-only staged chunk validation pass first.
- That root-only scanner was weaker than the event-reader path: it could accept
  a `<worksheet>` root shape that later only produced a relationship-audit parse
  note instead of failing the replacement.

Output:
- Removed the old full-worksheet root-only streaming validator from
  `package_editor.cpp`.
- `worksheet_replacement_audits_from_chunk_source()` now lets event-reader
  worksheet parse errors propagate as replacement failures, while
  relationship-id scanner failures remain audit notes.
- `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` now opens the staged worksheet chunks once
  for event-reader validation plus payload/relationship audit.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now covers a root-only-valid but event-invalid
  payload (`<worksheet><row .../></worksheet>`) and verifies no state pollution.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Complete worksheet staged replacement no longer has a separate root-only
  validation reader before the audit reader.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken target/workbook/calc preconditions or no-state-pollution.
- Do not turn relationship-id audit into relationship repair or pruning.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion; caller-provided complete
  worksheet XML is still staged before commit.

## P8.123 - Remove sheetData rewritten-output validation reread

Status: done.

Goal: remove the remaining post-output event-reader pass over the rewritten
worksheet staged by `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()`.
Source/planned worksheet input is already validated while the output writer
consumes it, and replacement `<sheetData>` chunks are already validated by the
combined replacement root/audit pass before output staging.
