- P8.120 moved relationship-id audit into the sheetData output writer, but the
  caller still reopened the rewritten worksheet staged chunk once for a
  validation-only event-reader pass.
- That extra pass did not provide new user-input validation: it only re-read the
  deterministic splice output after source worksheet and replacement sheetData
  validation had already succeeded.

Output:
- Removed the rewritten worksheet validation-only `PackageEntryChunkReader`
  block from `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()`.
- Kept the bounded rewritten-output size guard before commit at the time of this
  slice; P8.129 later removed that stale final-output-size failure after
  rewritten worksheet output became file-backed staged data.
- Updated the sheetData output note and docs to state that the staged rewritten
  worksheet is not reopened for a post-output validation/audit reread.
- Adjusted over-limit queued worksheet fixtures to use multiple bounded XML
  comment events rather than one oversized XML token, so the tests exercise the
  local rewrite size guard without relying on event-reader window failure.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green under the 60s CTest timeout.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- No temporary diagnostic output remains in `tests/test_package_editor.cpp`.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken source worksheet event validation or replacement `<sheetData>`
  root validation.
- Do not move payload-policy checks after output staging.
- Do not remove the bounded rewritten-output size guard in this historical slice;
  P8.129 supersedes this by removing only that final output-size guard while
  keeping replacement payload and event-reader window guards.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion.

## P8.124 - Fuse complete worksheet chunk-source staging and audit

Status: done.

Goal: remove the staged-chunk validation/audit reread from
`PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_part_from_chunk_source()`. A pull-based caller
should be consumed once after target/workbook/calc-policy preflight, with that
same pass writing the PackageEditor-owned staged file and validating/auditing the
worksheet XML before any Patch state mutation.

Input:
- P8.122 fused validation and payload/relationship audit for already staged
  worksheet chunks.
- The chunk-source complete worksheet helper still wrote caller chunks to a temp
  file, then delegated to `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()`, which reopened that
  just-created staged chunk for the fused validation/audit pass.
- This extra pass was not a materialized string fallback, but it was still a
  planned-input pass-count cost on the internal low-memory path.

Output:
- Added `write_worksheet_replacement_file_and_audit_from_chunk_source()`, which
  writes caller worksheet chunks to the staged file while feeding the same chunks
  through the worksheet event reader for root/events validation, payload
  dependency audit, and relationship-id audit.
- `replace_worksheet_part_from_chunk_source()` now commits via
  `replace_worksheet_part_prevalidated_chunks()` with those audit results,
  instead of delegating to `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` and reopening the
  staged chunk.
- Chunk-source replacement notes now state that staging, validation, and audit
  happen in one caller chunk-source pass without reopening the staged chunk.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` checks the new note on direct and follow-up
  planned-input chunk-source replacement paths.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green under the 60s CTest timeout.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Complete worksheet chunk-source replacement still preflights target/workbook/
  calc policy before consuming caller chunks, still cleans temp files on caller
  read failure, and still propagates payload/relationship audit state.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken explicit `replace_worksheet_part_chunks()` validation/audit; it
  still needs its own one-pass staged-chunk reader for caller-provided chunks.
- Do not weaken worksheet event validation, relationship-id audit propagation,
  payload-policy no-state-pollution, or calc metadata handling.
- Do not claim public low-memory editor completion; caller complete worksheet XML
  is still staged before commit, and public editor API decisions remain open.

## P8.125 - Reuse prevalidated cell replacement lookup plan

Status: done.

Goal: avoid rebuilding the cell-replacement selector lookup and reparsing each
caller-materialized replacement `<c>` payload across the analysis pass and the
dimension-refreshed output pass in `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells()`.

Input:
- P8.115 added `TrustPrevalidated`, but the span-based transformer overloads
  still built a fresh non-owning index for each pass.
- P8.117 fused relationship-id audit into the output pass, leaving two current
  worksheet input passes: dependency/dimension/payload analysis, then
  dimension-refreshed output with relationship-id audit.
- The two worksheet input passes are still intentional because missing selector
  diagnostics, dimension refresh, payload audit, relationship-id audit, policy
  rejection, and no-state-pollution are staged around them.

Output:
- Added internal `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan` plus
  `make_worksheet_cell_replacement_plan()` to build one prevalidated non-owning
  selector/payload lookup over caller-owned replacements.
- `scan_cell_replacement_actions_from_chunk_source()` and
  `emit_cell_replacement_worksheet_from_chunk_source()` consume that existing
  plan.
- `replace_worksheet_cells()` builds one plan before source/planned worksheet
  scanning and reuses it for both analysis and output.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` now proves the same plan can drive scan and
  emit; `fastxlsx.package_editor` asserts planned-output notes expose this
  lookup-plan reuse.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not collapse the two worksheet input passes unless dimension refresh,
  missing selector diagnostics, payload audit, relationship-id audit, policy
  ordering, and no-state-pollution are all preserved.
- Do not claim replacement cell XML is streamed; it remains a bounded
  caller-materialized single-cell payload.
- Do not expose this as public low-memory editor API.

## P8.126 - Delete transitional replacement trust mode and span overloads

Status: done.

Goal: remove the remaining internal compatibility-shaped transformer API after
P8.125 introduced explicit `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan` reuse. Cell
replacement callers must now build one validated plan and pass it to every
transformer scan/emit pass.

Input:
- P8.115 added a temporary trust mode to avoid payload reparsing.
- P8.125 introduced the real replacement lookup plan abstraction and
  `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells()` already uses it.
- The remaining span-based transformer overloads could still hide a fresh plan
  build per call and keep the trust-mode bypass alive.

Output:
- Removed `WorksheetReplacementPayloadValidationMode` and `TrustPrevalidated`
  from the internal transformer API.
- Removed span-based
  `scan_cell_replacement_actions_from_chunk_source()` /
  `emit_cell_replacement_worksheet_from_chunk_source()` convenience overloads.
- `make_worksheet_cell_replacement_plan()` now always validates selector
  presence, duplicate selectors, replacement payload size, non-empty payloads,
  cell root, unqualified `r`, and selector / payload reference match.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` helpers now explicitly build a plan before
  calling transformer scan/emit functions, and the old trust-mode regression was
  replaced by a test that invalid payloads cannot bypass plan preflight.

Acceptance:
- No source/header/test references to the removed validation-mode enum,
  trust-mode value, or span-based transformer overloads remain.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not reintroduce a validation bypass as a compatibility shim.
- Do not let transformer callers pass raw replacement spans directly to scan or
  emit functions.
- Do not claim replacement cell XML is streamed; it remains a bounded
  caller-materialized single-cell payload.
- Do not expose this as public low-memory editor API.

## P8.127 - Direct sheetData replacement insertion during local rewrite

Status: done.

Goal: remove the separate replacement `<sheetData>` temp-file staging/replay
from bounded sheetData local rewrite. The replacement chunks should be consumed
only when the source/planned worksheet output writer reaches the existing
`<sheetData>` element, without first materializing a standalone replacement
sheetData file-backed chunk.

Input:
- P8.47 introduced chunk-source sheetData replacement but initially staged the
  replacement payload separately.
- P8.119 and P8.120 fused relationship-id and preservation audit into the
  rewritten worksheet output writer.
- P8.121 fused replacement root validation and replacement payload audit, but
  still operated on the separate replacement staging boundary.

Output:
- Removed the separate `write_sheet_data_replacement_file_and_audit...` flow.
- Added direct insertion from caller `WorksheetInputChunkCallback` while
  `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()` writes the
  rewritten worksheet XML.
- Replacement `<sheetData>` root/structure validation, bounded payload-size
  guard, replacement payload dependency audit, relationship-id scan, and output
  insertion now happen in the same caller replacement chunk-source pass.
- The only successful staged payload from this helper is the rewritten worksheet
  XML, recorded as a PackageEditor-owned file-backed staged chunk for follow-up
  planned-input transforms.
- Payload policy failure can now occur after the scoped rewritten worksheet temp
  file has been written, but still before the file is released into editor-owned
  state; no `EditPlan`, manifest, calc policy, planned output, or temp-file
  ownership state is committed on failure.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` verifies the new notes:
  `consumed directly` and
  `without staging or replaying a separate replacement sheetData chunk`.
- Existing invalid replacement, throwing replacement chunk source, payload
  policy failure, over-limit replacement, and no-state-pollution tests remain
  green.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not remove the replacement payload guard or event-reader retained-window guard.
- Do not claim this is the full large-file streaming worksheet transformer or a
  public low-memory editor API.
- Do not weaken replacement payload audit, relationship-id audit, preservation
  audit, calc metadata handling, or unknown-part preservation.

## P8.128 - Explicit cell replacement payload view boundary

Status: done.

Goal: remove the internal naked string propagation for replacement `<c>`
payloads. P8.83/P8.109 made the remaining single-cell materialization boundary
explicit and bounded; this slice moves that boundary behind a dedicated payload
view so transformer actions, replacement plans, and PackageEditor audit/output
paths no longer carry raw replacement XML string fields as their state shape.

Input:
- P8.125 introduced reusable `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan`.
- P8.126 removed transformer span overloads and trust-mode validation bypasses.
- The remaining `materialized_replacement_cell_xml` fields still made the
  internal action/plan state look like direct string payload propagation.

Output:
- Added internal `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` with
  `from_materialized_xml()` plus chunk replay / preflight accessors.
- `WorksheetCellReplacement`, `WorksheetTransformAction`, and
  `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan` now store/pass `replacement_payload` /
  `replacement_payloads_by_reference` instead of raw replacement XML string
  fields.
- `PackageEditor` cell replacement payload audit and output writing now go
  through the payload view instead of storing raw replacement XML fields.
- Transformer and PackageEditor tests now construct replacements through
  explicit helper wrappers, so old `{selector, string}` aggregate initialization
  no longer compiles at call sites.
- `PackageEditor` planned-output notes expose the explicit payload-view boundary
  and later P8.162 updates that boundary to bounded caller-owned single-cell XML
  chunks with preflight-only materialization, still not a streamed cell payload
  source.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Source/test searches no longer find `WorksheetTransformAction` or
  `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan` carrying `materialized_replacement_cell_xml`
  as a raw field.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim replacement `<c>` payloads are truly streamed yet; this slice is
  a type-boundary cleanup for the remaining bounded cell-payload backing.
- Do not reintroduce `{cell_reference, std::string_view}` aggregate shorthand as
  a compatibility layer.
- Do not expose this as public low-memory editor API.

## P8.129 - Remove rewritten sheetData output-size guard

Status: done.

Goal: remove the stale final-output-size failure from bounded sheetData local
rewrite. The rewritten worksheet XML is already persisted as a
PackageEditor-owned file-backed staged chunk; rejecting it only because the
final output exceeds the replacement-payload guard kept a whole-worksheet-size
limit on a file-backed output path and conflicted with the planned-input
low-memory direction.

Input:
- P8.127 consumes replacement `<sheetData>` chunks directly during the
  rewritten worksheet output pass.
- The source/planned worksheet input path already uses chunk-source readers.
- The rewritten worksheet output already enters `EditPlan` as staged
  `PackageEntryChunk` data.

Output:
- Removed the rewritten worksheet XML final-size call to
  the old local-rewrite-named size helper; P8.130 later renamed that remaining
  helper boundary to `require_sheet_data_replacement_payload_size()`.
- Replacement `<sheetData>` payloads still fail before Patch state mutation
  when they exceed `package_editor_sheet_data_replacement_payload_byte_limit`.
- Oversized source/planned worksheet input can still fail through the
  event-reader retained-window guard for incomplete XML markup/text tokens.
- Added direct and by-name regressions proving source, queued staged, and
  queued staged-then-rewritten sheetData output may exceed the old payload
  guard while staying file-backed (`staged_replacement_chunks == true`,
  `materialized_replacement == false`) and preserving calcChain cleanup plus
  unknown extension bytes.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` covers rewritten-output-over-payload-limit success
  for source and planned staged worksheet input.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not call this a true large-file streaming worksheet transformer; the
  source/planned event-reader retained window and replacement payload guard
  still exist.
- Do not loosen replacement root validation, payload dependency audit,
  relationship-id audit, calc metadata handling, or unknown-part preservation.
- Do not add public low-memory editor API.

## P8.130 - Rename sheetData payload guard boundary

Status: done.

Goal: remove the misleading leftover `local_rewrite` name from the only
remaining sheetData size guard. After P8.129, that guard applies to caller
replacement `<sheetData>` payload chunks, not to the file-backed rewritten
worksheet output or the whole local rewrite.

Input:
- P8.129 removed the rewritten worksheet XML final-size guard.
- The remaining replacement payload guard still used a local-rewrite-oriented
  name, which made it look as if it bounded the full local rewrite.

Output:
- Renamed the internal constant to
  `package_editor_sheet_data_replacement_payload_byte_limit`.
- Renamed the internal helper to
  `require_sheet_data_replacement_payload_size()`.
- Updated over-limit error text to say `bounded payload limit`.
- Renamed the source-input over-limit regression to describe the event-reader
  retained-window boundary instead of a local-rewrite size boundary.
- Updated docs and task notes so replacement payload, event-reader input window,
  and file-backed rewritten output are three separate boundaries.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Source/docs searches no longer find the old local-rewrite-named sheetData
  payload limit symbol.

Forbidden:
- Do not change the numeric limit or broaden accepted replacement payloads.
- Do not remove the replacement payload guard or event-reader retained-window
  guard.
- Do not expose this as public low-memory editor API.

## P8.131 - PackageReader extract-to-file committed output state

Status: done.

Goal: harden the remaining internal `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()`
file-output helper so failed package entry extraction cannot leave a half-written
file that later file-backed chunk consumers might treat as valid input.

Input:
- P8.28/P8.29/P8.31/P8.108 unified stored/DEFLATE `entry_chunk_source()`
  foundations.
- Existing stored and minizip DEFLATE extraction and CRC failure regressions.

Output:
- `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()` now writes entry chunks to a sibling
  temporary file and commits that file to `output_path` only after chunk-source
  read, CRC/size validation, and output finalization have completed.
- Fresh, non-existing output paths are accepted; only an existing directory
  target is rejected before entry chunks are consumed.
- Failed read/CRC/write/finalize/commit paths best-effort remove the temporary
  file and preserve the previous output file.
- Existing output files are moved aside only during the final commit step, and
  restored if the final rename fails.
- Directory output targets are rejected before consuming the package entry
  chunk source, preserving the directory and its contents.
- Exact or path-equivalent source package output targets are rejected before
  consuming entry chunks, preventing accidental in-place package corruption.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers stored extraction to a fresh output path,
  stored extraction replacing stale output, stored corrupt CRC extraction
  preserving previous output, and directory output target rejection preserving
  existing directory contents.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` also covers direct and path-equivalent source package
  overwrite rejection and reopens the source package afterwards.
- The same failure-preserves-output behavior is covered for minizip DEFLATE
  builds by the existing corrupt DEFLATE extraction regression.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()` as public API.
- Do not claim this makes `read_entry()` low-memory; it still returns a
  materialized `std::string`.
- Do not claim this completes the C5 large worksheet transformer; this is only
  internal file-output state hygiene for package entry extraction.

## P8.132 - Replayable cell replacement payload view

Status: done.

Goal: narrow the remaining replacement `<c>` materialization boundary to
preflight only. P8.128 introduced `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload`, but audit
and output code could still treat the payload as a direct materialized string.
This slice makes transformer/package-editor consumers replay payload chunks so
future chunked payload backing can be introduced behind the same type boundary.

Input:
- P8.125 introduced reusable `WorksheetCellReplacementPlan`.
- P8.128 introduced `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload`.
- Cell replacement output is already file-backed staged chunks, so it should
  not need a direct materialized replacement payload field.

Output:
- `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` now exposes `byte_size()`, `empty()`, and
  `for_each_chunk()` for normal consumers.
- The direct materialized payload accessor was replaced by
  an explicit preflight-only materialization boundary; P8.162 later expanded the
  backing to bounded caller-provided single-cell XML chunks.
- Transformer output emission replays replacement payload chunks instead of
  emitting a raw materialized string.
- PackageEditor cell replacement output writer replays replacement payload
  chunks while scanning relationships.
- PackageEditor cell-payload audit now scans replayed payload chunks through a
  bounded tag-window helper instead of taking a direct full string view.
- Transformer tests now collect replacement payload XML by replaying chunks,
  not by calling a materialized payload accessor.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remains green.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` remains green.
- Full default CTest remains green.
- Source/test searches show no `.materialized_xml()` payload accessor usage;
  only the explicit preflight materialization boundary remains.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim replacement `<c>` payloads are fully streamed; the current backing
  is still bounded caller-provided single-cell XML chunks, with preflight-only
  materialization.
- Do not remove the single-cell materialization limit or preflight validation.
- Do not expose this as public low-memory editor API.

## P8.133 - PackageEditor save_as committed output state

Status: done.

Goal: harden the final `PackageEditor::save_as()` output package boundary so
writer/backend failures or copy-original source-copy failures cannot overwrite a
valid caller output file or leave sibling temporary package files behind.

Input:
- P8.50/P8.54/P8.103 file-backed save-time source-copy boundary.
- P8.131 `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()` success-only commit boundary.
- Existing `save_as()` guard and failure no-state-pollution regressions.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::save_as()` now materializes planned output entries first, then
  writes the assembled package to a target-directory sibling temporary path.
- The target output path is only updated after `write_package()` succeeds and
  `commit_package_editor_output_file()` performs the final rename.
- Existing output files are moved aside only during final commit and restored if
  the final rename fails.
- Copy-original source-copy temporary XML files are tracked separately and
  cleaned after successful or failed `save_as()`.
- Writer/backend failures and copy-original read/CRC failures remove output
  sibling temps, preserve any existing output bytes, and keep the queued
  `EditPlan`, manifest, package-entry audits, payload audits, relationship
  audits, calc policy, and planned output reusable.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` covers copy-original read failure preserving the
  existing output file, cleaning staged source-copy temps and committed-output
  sibling temps, preserving queued state, and succeeding with the same editor
  after the source package bytes are restored.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` covers writer/backend failure preserving the existing
  output file, cleaning staged source-copy temps and committed-output sibling
  temps, preserving queued state, and succeeding on a later safe `save_as()`.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers fresh and stale extraction outputs used by
  save-time source-copy staging.
- Full default CTest remains green.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `PackageEditor` as public API from this slice.
- Do not claim crash-proof filesystem transactions or in-place editing; this is
  a best-effort sibling-temp success-only commit boundary.
- Do not claim this completes the C5 large worksheet transformer or a full
  low-memory package writer; active replacements and workbook small-XML helpers
  still have explicit materialized boundaries.

## P8.134 - PackageReader extract output parent preflight

Status: done.

Goal: harden the remaining internal `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()`
target-path validation so invalid output parents fail before any package entry
chunk source is consumed.

Input:
- P8.131 committed-output extraction boundary.
- Existing corrupt-entry extraction regressions that prove chunk-source CRC
  failures preserve previous output.

Output:
- `validate_extraction_output_target()` now requires a non-empty parent path to
  already exist and be a directory.
- Missing parent and non-directory parent paths fail before temporary extraction
  file creation and before entry chunk reads / CRC validation.
- Fresh output filenames are still accepted when their parent directory exists.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers missing-parent and file-parent extraction
  targets against a source package with a corrupt target entry; the observed
  failure is the parent-path preflight, proving the entry chunk source was not
  consumed.
- Existing fresh/stale/directory/source-overwrite extraction tests remain green.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()` as public API.
- Do not reject fresh output filenames whose parent directory exists.
- Do not claim this completes low-memory worksheet editing; this is only
  extraction target-state hygiene.

## P8.135 - PackageReader read-entry materialization size contract

Status: done.

Goal: harden the remaining `PackageReader::read_entry()` materialization adapter
so the unified chunk-source path cannot silently return a string whose byte count
differs from the ZIP entry metadata.

Input:
- P8.32 / P8.108 `read_entry()` chunk-source materialization handoff.
- Stored and minizip DEFLATE chunk sources already validate real package CRC/size.
- Existing C5 rule that `read_entry()` still returns a full `std::string` and
  is not a low-memory public API.

Output:
- `read_entry_chunks_to_string()` now tracks emitted bytes while consuming the
  chunk source.
- It rejects empty chunks, chunk streams that end before `expected_size`, and
  chunk streams that produce more than `expected_size`.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS`-only internal test hook exposes this helper to
  `fastxlsx.package_reader` without changing the production API surface.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers a valid multi-chunk materialization and the
  empty-chunk / short-stream / overrun failure paths.
- Existing stored and DEFLATE read-entry / chunk-source / CRC regressions remain
  green.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim `PackageReader::read_entry()` is low-memory; it still materializes
  the complete entry as `std::string`.
- Do not expose this test hook as public API.
- Do not treat arbitrary external chunk sources as a public extension point.

## P8.136 - PackageReader extract-entry size contract before commit

Status: done.

Goal: harden `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()` so file-backed extraction
does not commit a sibling temporary file unless the consumed chunk source emits
exactly the expected entry byte count.

Input:
- P8.131 / P8.134 committed-output and output-target preflight boundaries.
- P8.135 shared chunk-source size-contract helper for materialized reads.
- Existing extraction CRC/read failure tests that preserve previous output bytes.

Output:
- `extract_entry_chunks_to_file()` now receives `expected_size`, tracks written
  bytes, and uses the shared empty-chunk / short-stream / overrun validation
  before finalizing and committing the extraction output.
- `PackageReader::extract_entry_to_file()` passes the source entry
  `uncompressed_size` into the committed-file helper.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS`-only helper covers exact-size commit and
  failure-before-commit behavior with synthetic chunk sources.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers exact-size extraction plus empty-chunk,
  short-stream and overrun failures preserving the previous output file.
- Existing fresh/stale extraction, invalid output target, source-overwrite, and
  CRC failure regressions remain green.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose extraction helpers as public API.
- Do not claim filesystem transaction guarantees beyond the current best-effort
  sibling-temp success-only commit boundary.
- Do not claim this completes low-memory worksheet editing; this is PackageReader
  file-backed extraction hygiene only.

## P8.137 - PackageEntryChunk staged payload size contract

Status: done.

Goal: harden internal staged chunk payloads so a file-backed
`PackageEntryChunk` cannot be validated at queue time and then silently read
with a different byte count during planned-input worksheet transforms or
package output.

Input:
- P8.26 / P8.85 planned staged-chunk worksheet input through
  `PackageEntryChunkReader` / `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader`.
- P8.133 `PackageEditor::save_as()` committed-output boundary.
- P8.135 / P8.136 chunk-source size-contract pattern.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunk` now carries internal expected-size metadata after staged
  chunk validation.
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` validates that file chunks still match the recorded
  size before and while reading, and rejects short or overrun reads.
- Stored and minizip package writers validate the same expected-size metadata
  when computing/writing chunked package entries.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` covers planned worksheet staged file chunks that are
  truncated or extended before a follow-up cell replacement; both failures occur
  before state mutation and do not leak PackageEditor temp files.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` also covers generic staged file chunks that are
  truncated or extended before `save_as()`; output bytes, queued edit state, and
  later safe save remain intact.
- Existing staged chunk, source-copy, and worksheet replacement regressions stay
  green.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `PackageEntryChunk` expected-size metadata as public API.
- Do not claim content immutability or CRC guarantees for same-size file
  changes; this slice enforces byte-count contract only.
- Do not claim this completes low-memory large-file editing.

## P8.138 - Contextualize current worksheet chunk-source read failures

Status: done.

Goal: make source-entry and planned staged-chunk read failures in the shared
current-worksheet input reader identify the exact PackageEditor pass that was
consuming the worksheet, while preserving the original ZIP/staged-chunk error.

Input:
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` shared by sheetData replacement and cell
  replacement analysis/output passes.
- P8.137 staged chunk expected-size errors and PackageReader source-entry CRC /
  size errors.

Output:
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` now takes a purpose string and wraps
  initialization / read failures with that boundary.
- Cell replacement passes use distinct analysis/output purposes; sheetData
  replacement uses an output purpose.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` corrupts a stored source worksheet entry after
  `PackageEditor::open()`, then verifies cell replacement fails with both the
  current worksheet analysis boundary and the underlying ZIP CRC mismatch.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` also covers the same post-open source worksheet
  corruption through bounded sheetData replacement, verifying the sheetData
  output boundary and underlying ZIP CRC mismatch are both visible.
- Existing planned staged-chunk size-mutation coverage now also verifies the
  current worksheet input read boundary is preserved alongside the
  expected-size failure detail for both cell replacement and bounded sheetData
  replacement follow-up paths.
- The same regression verifies no edit-plan entries, notes, payload audits,
  relationship audits, calc policy, manifest write mode, or PackageEditor temp
  files are polluted.

Forbidden:
- Do not hide the underlying ZIP or staged chunk failure detail.
- Do not add public editor API or claim complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.139 - Contextualize staged package-entry chunk diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: make staged package-entry chunk failures identify the exact chunk that
failed, so C5 planned-input and save/output paths can keep their higher-level
PackageEditor context while still exposing the concrete chunk boundary.

Input:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` and staged chunk preflight helpers used by
  worksheet staged replacements, generic staged package-part replacements,
  current planned worksheet input, and save-time chunked output.
- P8.137 expected-size validation and P8.138 current worksheet input failure
  context.

Output:
- Staged chunk preflight and reader failures now include
  `staged package-entry chunk N`, with file-backed chunks also reporting the
  file path.
- Existing higher-level context remains intact: generic/worksheet staged
  replacement preflight, current worksheet input analysis/output, sheetData
  output, and `save_as()` still wrap the underlying chunk failure with their
  operation boundary.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` verifies invalid generic staged chunks and invalid
  worksheet staged chunks report the failing chunk index without mutating
  edit-plan state.
- Planned staged-chunk size-mutation regressions for both cell replacement and
  bounded sheetData replacement now also verify the file-backed chunk index and
  file path are visible alongside the current worksheet input boundary and
  expected-size detail.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose chunk descriptors or expected-size metadata as public API.
- Do not claim same-size payload immutability, relationship repair, or complete
  low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.140 - Contextualize PackageWriter staged chunk preflight failures

Status: done.

Goal: preserve entry/chunk context when final package assembly rejects invalid
or changed staged chunks, so `PackageEditor::save_as()` diagnostics can point to
the output entry and chunk that failed.

Input:
- `PackageWriter` ZIP32 preflight for chunked `PackageEntry` payloads.
- `PackageEditor::save_as()` staged chunk output paths that hand active
  replacements and file-backed source-copy entries to `PackageWriter`.

Output:
- `PackageWriter` staged chunk preflight failures now include
  `ZIP entry '<name>' chunk N`; file-backed chunks also include the chunk path.
- Existing `PackageEditor::save_as()` wrapping remains intact, so callers still
  see the output package path plus the lower-level entry/chunk failure.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` verifies invalid package-writer chunk sources name
  the failing output entry and chunk.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` verifies save-time staged chunk size mutation names
  the output entry, chunk index, file-backed chunk path, and original size
  contract detail while preserving edit state and output bytes.

Forbidden:
- Do not change ZIP backend selection, chunk payload semantics, or public API.
- Do not claim this adds Zip64, atomic in-place editing, or same-size content
  immutability.

## P8.141 - Contextualize stored ZIP backend chunk failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep the same entry/chunk diagnostics when the stored bootstrap ZIP
backend fails while computing chunk CRC/size or while writing chunk bytes, not
only during `PackageWriter` preflight.

Input:
- P8.140 `PackageWriter` staged chunk preflight context.
- Stored bootstrap backend `compute_entry_stats()` and `write_entry_data()`
  paths in `src/zip_store_writer.cpp`.
- Existing staged chunk expected-size contract metadata.

Output:
- Stored backend chunk failures now include `ZIP entry '<name>' chunk N` during
  actual backend CRC/size computation and data writing.
- File-backed chunk failures also include the file-backed chunk path and still
  preserve the lower-level size/read/write detail.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers a direct stored-backend regression where a
  file-backed chunk grows after validation metadata was recorded; the failure
  names the output entry, chunk index, chunk path, and byte-count contract
  detail.

Forbidden:
- Do not change ZIP backend selection, chunk payload semantics, Zip64 policy,
  or public API.
- Do not claim this makes `write_stored_zip()` an atomic commit boundary or
  proves same-size file content immutability.

## P8.142 - Keep monolithic package_editor CTest runnable

Status: done.

Goal: restore default CTest usability after the accumulated `fastxlsx.package_editor`
preservation / C5 regression suite outgrew the old 60s timeout.

Input:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now passes as a direct executable but takes roughly
  four minutes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.
- Most other default tests still complete under the existing 60s timeout.

Output:
- `tests/CMakeLists.txt` keeps the 60s timeout for ordinary targets, but sets
  `fastxlsx.package_editor` to a 300s timeout.
- Testing docs now call out this target as a monolithic preservation-suite
  timeout exception; this temporary state is superseded by P8.143 sharding.
- P8.143 supersedes this temporary exception by splitting the CTest registration
  into smaller `fastxlsx.package_editor.*` shards.

Acceptance:
- Direct `fastxlsx_package_editor_tests.exe` passes within the documented 300s
  bound.
- Full default CTest can run without the previous 60s false timeout on
  `fastxlsx.package_editor`.

Forbidden:
- Do not present the timeout increase as a performance improvement.
- Do not move benchmarks into default CTest.
- Do not use this timeout exception to justify further unbounded growth; the
  follow-up package_editor test sharding is tracked by P8.143.

## P8.143 - Shard package_editor CTest registration

Status: done.

Goal: replace the temporary monolithic `fastxlsx.package_editor` CTest timeout
exception with smaller CTest shards while keeping the existing single test
source and executable.

Input:
- P8.142 restored full default CTest by giving the monolithic preservation /
  C5 regression suite a 300s timeout.
- The accumulated `tests/test_package_editor.cpp` coverage now spans C5
  chunk-source/file-backed handoff, preservation fixtures, sheetData patch,
  planned catalog, and policy/guard failure paths.

Output:
- `tests/test_package_editor.cpp` accepts `--shard=<name>` and validates shard
  names before running tests.
- `tests/CMakeLists.txt` registers six default CTest entries:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.core`,
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5`,
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.preservation-core`,
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.preservation-linked`,
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata`, and
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.policy`.
- The old monolithic CTest entry is no longer part of default CTest; the same
  executable can still run all package editor tests directly when no shard is
  passed.
- All package editor shards use the normal 60s timeout again.

Acceptance:
- `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_package_editor_tests` succeeds.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.package_editor
  --output-on-failure` runs all package editor shards and each shard stays under
  the 60s CTest timeout on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.
- Full default CTest no longer depends on a single 300s package_editor
  exception.

Forbidden:
- Do not move package_editor tests into benchmark targets.
- Do not present sharding as runtime performance work; this is test
  maintainability and CI reliability work.
- Do not use the shard names as public API or production feature boundaries.

## P8.144 - Contextualize minizip backend chunk write failures

Status: done.

Goal: align the opt-in minizip writer backend with the stored bootstrap backend
when actual chunk writes fail after package-level preflight.

Input:
- P8.140 added output entry/chunk context to `PackageWriter` staged chunk
  preflight.
- P8.141 added stored backend entry/chunk/path context to actual CRC/size and
  write stages.
- The minizip backend still wrote chunk payloads without wrapping

Output:

  memory/file chunk write failures through `wrap_zip_entry_chunk_error()`.
- Unsupported chunk kinds encountered at the backend write stage report the same
  `ZIP entry '<name>' chunk N` context, including `(unknown)`.
- No public API, ZIP format policy, compression-level behavior, or dependency
  wiring changes.

Acceptance:
- Default `windows-nmake-release` build and CTest remain green.
- Opt-in minizip preset should continue to validate minizip output/backend

Forbidden:
- Do not present this as broad minizip production readiness or as Zip64/data
  descriptor support.
- Do not weaken package writer preflight; this is backend-stage diagnostic
  hardening only.

## P8.145 - Add expected/actual byte counts to chunk size diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: make C5 staged chunk and package-reader chunk-source size-contract

## P8.147 - Bind save-time source-copy temp chunks to source entry size contracts

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 save-time copy-original handoff file-backed all the way through
`PackageWriter`, and make any source-copy temporary file size mutation fail with
the same entry/chunk diagnostics as staged replacements.

Input:
- P8.79/P8.103/P8.104 source-copy materialization cleanup and file-backed
  source-copy output.
- P8.137 staged chunk expected-size validation.
- P8.140/P8.145/P8.146 save-time entry/chunk/materialization diagnostics.

Output:

  `PackageReaderEntry` and sets the temporary file-backed `PackageEntryChunk`
  `expected_size` to the source entry `uncompressed_size` before handing it to
  `PackageWriter`.
- If a source-copy temp file is truncated or extended after extraction but
  before writer preflight, the package writer rejects it through the existing
  `ZIP entry '<name>' chunk N` expected/actual-size path instead of reading to
  EOF silently.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS`-only save-time hook lets regression tests
  mutate source-copy temp files between materialization and package writing; it
  is not compiled as public API behavior.
- No source-copy temp lifecycle, output-plan shape, ZIP backend selection,
  public API, or low-memory capability claim changed.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` verifies a truncated source-copy temp file fails
  before output commit, reports PackageEditor output context plus ZIP
  entry/chunk/path and expected/actual size diagnostics, cleans source-copy and
  output sibling temp files, preserves queued edit state, and allows a later
  safe save.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_editor"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose source-copy temp paths, hooks, or expected-size metadata as
  public API.
- Do not claim same-size payload immutability, atomic in-place save,
  relationship repair, broad preservation proof, or complete low-memory
  worksheet editing.

## P8.148 - Bind save-time source-copy temp chunks to source entry CRC contracts

Status: done.

Goal: extend the P8.147 source-copy temp contract from byte count to payload
identity, so a save-time copy-original temp file rewritten with the same size is
also rejected before output commit.

Input:
- P8.147 source-copy temp expected-size contract.
- `PackageReaderEntry::crc32` metadata from the source ZIP entry.
- Existing stored/minizip writer entry/chunk/path diagnostics.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunk` now carries optional internal `expected_crc32` metadata in
  addition to `expected_size`; this remains internal writer state, not public
  API.
- `PackageEditor::save_as()` source-copy materialization copies both
  `uncompressed_size` and `crc32` from the source `PackageReaderEntry` onto the
  temporary file-backed chunk before handing it to `PackageWriter`.
- Stored bootstrap package writing validates expected CRC during file-backed
  chunk stats calculation and again while writing file-backed chunk bytes.
- Opt-in minizip package writing validates expected CRC while streaming
  file-backed chunks into minizip.
- A policy-shard regression mutates source-copy temp files to different bytes
  with the same length and verifies `ZIP entry '<name>' chunk N` CRC diagnostics,
  existing-output preservation, source/output temp cleanup, queued edit-state
  hygiene, and a later safe save.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` verifies same-size source-copy temp mutation fails
  with PackageEditor output context, ZIP entry/chunk/path context, and
  expected/actual CRC diagnostics.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_editor"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose CRC metadata, source-copy temp paths, or test hooks as public
  API.
- Do not claim cryptographic tamper resistance, atomic in-place save,
  relationship repair, broad preservation proof, or complete low-memory
  worksheet editing.

## P8.149 - Bind queued staged chunks to CRC contracts

Status: done.

Goal: extend staged `PackageEntryChunk` validation beyond byte count so queued
file-backed staged payloads cannot be rewritten to different same-size bytes
without being detected by follow-up planned-input readers or save-time writers.

Input:
- P8.137 staged chunk expected-size metadata.
- P8.148 optional `PackageEntryChunk` expected-CRC metadata and writer-side CRC
  validation.
- Existing planned staged chunk input and save-time staged chunk failure
  regressions.

Output:
- `PackageEditor` staged chunk validation now computes and records
  `expected_crc32` for memory/file chunks at queue/preflight time.
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` validates staged chunk CRC when expected CRC metadata
  is first recorded, and when file-backed chunks are consumed, preserving the
  existing staged chunk index/path and current worksheet input purpose context.
- Save-time generic staged chunks reuse the writer-side expected-CRC checks, so
  same-size file-backed staged chunk mutation fails before output commit.
- New `fastxlsx.package_editor` regressions cover same-size CRC mutation for
  save-time generic staged chunks, planned staged chunk cell replacement input,
  and planned staged chunk sheetData follow-up input.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` verifies staged chunk CRC failures preserve queued
  edit state, output bytes, output/source temp hygiene, and planned staged
  worksheet state.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_editor"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose staged chunk CRC metadata as public API.
- Do not claim cryptographic tamper resistance, relationship repair, broad
  preservation proof, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.150 - Contextualize source-entry chunk-source CRC diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: make C5 source-entry chunk-source CRC failures as actionable as staged
chunk and writer failures, so corrupt stored source-entry bytes keep the entry
name and expected/actual CRC when reached through `read_entry()`, extraction,
raw chunk-source iteration, or PackageEditor current-worksheet planned-input
paths.

Input:
- P8.131/P8.135 PackageReader chunk-source read/extract adapters.
- P8.145 expected/actual diagnostic pattern.
- P8.146 current worksheet input failure context preservation.

Output:
- Stored source-entry chunk-source CRC mismatch now reports
  `ZIP entry '<name>' CRC mismatch: expected <crc>, actual <crc>`.
- The opt-in minizip chunk-source CRC close/read path reuses the same
  expected/actual CRC diagnostic when minizip reports a CRC mismatch.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` stored CRC regressions now verify entry name plus
  expected/actual CRC diagnostics for direct `read_entry()`,
  `extract_entry_to_file()`, and raw chunk-source consumption.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` current worksheet source-read failure regressions
  now verify the PackageEditor operation boundary preserves the underlying
  corrupt worksheet entry name and expected/actual CRC diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_(reader|editor)"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not change public `PackageReader::read_entry()` behavior; it still
  materializes a `std::string`.
- Do not claim cryptographic tamper resistance, compressed-input streaming
  completion, relationship repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.151 - Contextualize stored source-entry chunk-source IO failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 source-entry chunk-source diagnostics concrete even when the
stored package file fails at the open/seek/read IO layer after `PackageReader`
has already indexed the package.

Input:
- P8.150 source-entry CRC diagnostics with entry name.
- Existing stored source-entry chunk-source open/seek/read path.
- PackageReader tests that mutate package bytes after indexing.

Output:
- Stored source-entry chunk-source open/seek/read failures now report
  `ZIP entry '<name>': <io failure>`.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` adds a truncation-after-open regression proving
  `read_entry()` preserves the corrupt entry name and underlying read failure.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_reader"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not change public `PackageReader::read_entry()` behavior; it still
  materializes a `std::string`.
- Do not claim complete source-package streaming, relationship repair, or
  complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.152 - Contextualize DEFLATE source-entry chunk-source failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep opt-in minizip DEFLATE source-entry failures as actionable as stored
entry failures, so corrupt compressed source entries preserve the concrete ZIP
entry boundary when reached through direct `read_entry()`, committed extraction,
or raw chunk-source iteration.

Input:
- P8.150 source-entry CRC diagnostics with entry name.
- P8.151 stored source-entry IO failure context.
- Existing minizip DEFLATE source-entry chunk-source, extract, and read-entry
  materialization handoff.

Output:
- DEFLATE chunk-source open/locate/info/open/read/close failures now preserve
  `ZIP entry '<name>': <failure>` context unless the underlying diagnostic is
  already entry-scoped.
- DEFLATE CRC mismatch paths reuse the entry-scoped expected/actual CRC
  diagnostic.
- Direct `read_entry()` and `extract_entry_to_file()` now also wrap helper-level
  chunk materialization / extraction size-contract failures with the source
  entry name, covering failures outside the callback itself.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` minizip regressions verify corrupt DEFLATE
  `read_entry()`, `extract_entry_to_file()`, and raw `entry_chunk_source()`
  failures report `custom/blob.bin`.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release-minizip -R "fastxlsx.package_reader"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.
- Default `fastxlsx.package_reader` still passes, proving stored path context
  remains compatible.

Forbidden:
- Do not change public `PackageReader::read_entry()` behavior; it still
  materializes a `std::string`.
- Do not claim complete compressed-input streaming, low-memory `read_entry()`,
  relationship repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.153 - Contextualize missing source-entry lookup failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 source-entry diagnostics concrete when callers request an entry
that is not in the indexed package, before any chunk-source callback or output
target handling starts.

Input:
- P8.150-P8.152 entry-scoped source-entry CRC / IO / DEFLATE diagnostics.
- Existing `PackageReader::read_entry()`, `entry_chunk_source()`, and
  `extract_entry_to_file()` lookup path.

Output:
- Missing entry lookup now reports
  `ZIP entry '<name>' is not present in the package`.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers missing-entry diagnostics for direct
  `read_entry()`, raw `entry_chunk_source()`, and `extract_entry_to_file()`.
- Failed missing-entry extraction does not modify an existing output file.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_reader"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public package-editing API.
- Do not claim relationship repair, source-package streaming completion, or
  complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.154 - Contextualize unsupported compression method failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep unsupported ZIP compression-method failures actionable by naming the
source ZIP entry and method number before any PackageReader object, chunk-source
callback, or PackageEditor handoff exists.

Input:
- P8.150-P8.153 entry-scoped source-entry diagnostics.
- Existing central-directory compression-method validation in
  `PackageReader::open()`.
- Default build still rejects compressed input without minizip-ng; minizip build
  only adds DEFLATE support, not arbitrary compression methods.

Output:
- Unsupported compression failures now report
  `ZIP entry '<name>': unsupported ZIP compression method <N>; ...`.
- The default build still reports that only stored ZIP entries are supported
  without minizip-ng.
- The minizip build still reports that only stored or DEFLATE ZIP entries are
  supported.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` covers a method-12 entry and checks both the entry
  name and method number in the diagnostic under default and minizip presets.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_reader"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release-minizip -R "fastxlsx.package_reader"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not add new compression support.
- Do not change public `PackageReader::read_entry()` behavior; it still
  materializes a `std::string`.
- Do not claim complete compressed-input streaming, relationship repair, or
  complete low-memory worksheet editing.

Goal: keep central-directory to local-header validation failures actionable by
preserving the source ZIP entry name when `PackageReader::open()` rejects a
bad local file header.

## P8.157 - Contextualize invalid central-directory entry-name failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep `PackageReader::open()` invalid ZIP entry-name failures actionable by
showing the rejected central-directory entry name without reintroducing unsafe
raw control bytes into diagnostics.

- Shared `validate_zip_entry_name()` guard for reader/writer ZIP entry names.

Output:

- Entry diagnostics now escape backslashes, null bytes, and other control bytes

## P8.158 - Cover empty central-directory entry-name diagnostics
