## P8.352 - Wire private WorkbookEditor materialized-session state

Status: done.

Type: internal implementation + WorkbookEditor regression + docs; no public
API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no package
format expansion.

Goal: attach the internal materialized worksheet session registry to
`WorkbookEditor::Impl` so the future `WorksheetEditor` owner state has a real
private home before any public header is opened.

Output:
- `WorkbookEditor::Impl` now owns a
  `detail::MaterializedWorksheetSessionRegistry`.
- `replace_sheet_data()` now preflights the current planned sheet against that
  private registry and rejects the same planned sheet after internal
  materialization, before building replacement payload or queuing public edit
  state.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` has test-hook-only coverage that materializes one
  source sheet, marks it dirty, verifies move construction and move assignment
  transfer / replace the private registry with the rest of `Impl`, and verifies
  same-sheet whole-`<sheetData>` replacement is blocked while another sheet
  remains editable.

Non-goals / boundary:
- The test hooks are guarded by `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS`; they are not
  public API and do not create `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`,
  or `erase_cell()` public surface.
- Dirty materialized sessions are still not saved by public `save_as()`; this
  slice proves private ownership and operation-mixing hygiene only.
- No sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair, XML/schema repair,
  formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, in-place save, or large-file random
  editing is introduced.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` covers private registry move hygiene and
  operation-mixing preflight through test hooks.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx` outside `detail`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.353 - Add internal materialized-session flush handoff smoke

Status: done.

Type: internal test-hook implementation + WorkbookEditor regression + docs; no
public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no
package format expansion.

Goal: prove dirty private materialized sessions can be explicitly projected
through the existing by-name full worksheet chunk-source Patch helper before any
public random-edit facade is exposed.

Output:
- Added a `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS`-only
  `testing_workbook_editor_flush_materialized_sessions_to_patch_plan()` helper.
- The helper enumerates dirty private materialized sessions, passes each full
  worksheet chunk source to
  `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_part_from_chunk_source_by_name()`, and
  clears the private dirty flag only after that staged handoff succeeds.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now verifies clean materialization flush remains a
  no-op source roundtrip, while dirty materialized state flushes into the Patch
  plan and then `save_as()` persists the projected worksheet with refreshed
  sparse-store dimension and untouched-sheet byte preservation.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This is an internal explicit flush smoke, not automatic public
  `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` materialized-session persistence.
- It does not expose `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`,
  public `set_cell()`, or public `erase_cell()`.
- It does not migrate sharedStrings/styles, repair relationships, update ranges
  or linked objects semantically, evaluate formulas, rebuild calcChain, or
  provide large-file random editing.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` covers clean flush no-op behavior, dirty flush
  persistence via existing `save_as()`, dirty-flag clearing, refreshed
  dimension output, and untouched worksheet preservation.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx` outside `detail`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.354 - Wire materialized-session rename operation-mixing guard

Status: done.

Type: internal implementation + WorkbookEditor regression + docs; no public
API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no package
format expansion.

future first-slice reject-first design.

  against the private `MaterializedWorksheetSessionRegistry`.

  before catalog mutation, pending public edit mutation, or dirty-session
  changes.

  private session, dirty state, planned catalog, and public pending counters,
  while a different sheet can still be renamed and saved.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not expose materialization through public API; only test hooks can
  create the internal materialized state today.
- Dirty materialized cells still do not persist without the explicit internal
  flush hook from P8.353.
- No sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair, semantic range update,
  formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, in-place save, or large-file random
  editing is introduced.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` covers same-sheet rename rejection and different
  sheet rename success after internal materialization.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx` outside `detail`.

- The same regression sets source `A1` to explicit `CellValue::blank()`, erases
  existing source `A2`, flushes the materialized projection, and verifies output
  keeps explicit blank `A1`, preserves source `B1`, removes erased `A2` / row 2,
  and refreshes dimension to the remaining extents.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not expose public `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()` or public
  `WorksheetEditor` handles.
- It does not define tombstone semantics, style-preserving clear, row
  insertion/deletion, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair,
  formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, or range metadata recalculation.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.359 - Cover repeated materialization preserving dirty state

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + internal test-hook handle-reacquire evidence;

no package format expansion.

Goal: prove repeated materialization for the same planned sheet reuses the
existing private session and does not discard dirty edits by reloading source
worksheet cells.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now materializes source `Data`, mutates private
  materialized `A1`, materializes `Data` again through the same test hook, and
  verifies one session remains dirty.
- The flush/save output contains the dirty payload and does not revert to the
  original source `A1` text.

## P8.360 - Cover materialized source-load guard failure state hygiene

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + internal test-hook load guard hygiene; no
public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no
package format expansion.

Goal: prove a guarded private materialized source load fails before it pollutes
`WorkbookEditor` materialized registry, public pending diagnostics, or public
last-edit diagnostics.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` opens a source workbook with
  `WorkbookEditorOptions::max_replacement_cells = 1`, then attempts to
  materialize source `Data` through the test hook. Source `Data` has more than
  one cell, so the internal `CellStoreOptions` guard rejects the load.
- The regression verifies no private session is registered, no dirty private
  state is created, public pending counts remain zero, and `last_edit_error()`
  remains unset.
- The same editor then successfully queues a valid one-cell public
  `replace_sheet_data()` and saves it, proving the failed internal load did not
  poison later public facade use.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not expose public materialization options or public
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`.
- Current `WorkbookEditorOptions` remain public replacement-payload guardrails;
  they are reused here only because the current test hook maps them into the
  private `CellStoreOptions` bridge.
- No sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair, formula evaluation,
  calcChain rebuild, row operation, or range metadata recalculation is added.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.361 - Cover materialized source-load memory-budget failure state hygiene

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + internal test-hook memory guard hygiene; no
public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no
package format expansion.

Goal: prove the private materialized source-load memory-budget guard has the
same no-state-pollution behavior as the cell-count guard.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` opens a source workbook with
  `WorkbookEditorOptions::replacement_memory_budget_bytes = 1`, then attempts
  to materialize source `Data` through the test hook. The internal
  `CellStoreOptions::memory_budget_bytes` guard rejects the load.
- The regression verifies no private session is registered, no dirty private
  state is created, public pending diagnostics remain unchanged, and
  `last_edit_error()` remains unset.
- The same editor then successfully queues a catalog-only `rename_sheet()` and
  saves it, proving the failed internal load did not poison later public facade
  use.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not expose public `WorksheetEditorOptions` or public random-cell
  materialization.
- The budget is an internal CellStore estimate used by the current test-hook
  bridge, not exact process RSS tracking and not a future public memory
  contract.

Validation note:

  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.362 - Cover materialized source-load missing-sheet failure state hygiene

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + internal test-hook source catalog failure
hygiene; no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell
editing, and no package format expansion.

Goal: prove the private materialized source-load path fails cleanly when the
requested source sheet is absent from the source workbook catalog.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` opens a normal two-sheet source workbook, then
  attempts to materialize planned `Missing` from source sheet `Missing` through
  the test hook.
- The regression verifies that the failed load registers no private session,
  leaves no orphan session for the requested planned name, keeps dirty
  materialized state clean, leaves public pending diagnostics unchanged, and
  leaves public `last_edit_error()` unset.
- The same editor then successfully queues a public `replace_sheet_data()` and
  saves it, proving the failed internal source lookup did not poison later
  public facade use.

  `erase_cell()`, public source-sheet materialization, or public random-cell
  persistence.

  current internal test-hook path still permits non-catalog planned names so

- This does not add sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair,
  calcChain rebuild, formula evaluation, XML/schema repair, or automatic public
  `save_as()` flush behavior.

## P8.363 - Guard materialization after queued public sheetData replacement

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + internal operation-mixing guard; no public
API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no package
format expansion.

Goal: prevent the internal source-materialization test hook from reloading
source worksheet cells for a planned sheet that already has public
`replace_sheet_data()` payload queued.

Output:
- `WorkbookEditor` test-hook materialization now checks
  `pending_sheet_data_payloads` before source loading. If the planned sheet name
  already has a queued public replacement payload, it fails before
  `MaterializedWorksheetSessionRegistry` mutation.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` covers the same-sheet failure path: no private
  materialized session is registered, no dirty private state is created, queued
  public replacement diagnostics remain intact, and public `last_edit_error()`
  remains unset.
- The same test verifies a queued replacement on `Data` does not block
  materializing `Untouched`, and final `save_as()` still writes the queued
  `Data` replacement while preserving untouched worksheet bytes.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not make materialization read from current staged replacement
  payloads. The conservative policy is reject-first until public
  `WorksheetEditor` save/flush semantics are finalized.
- This does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, public `set_cell()` /
  `erase_cell()`, automatic public dirty-session flushing, relationship repair,
  sharedStrings/style migration, calcChain rebuild, formula evaluation, or
  XML/schema repair.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.364 - Guard materialization after renamed queued replacement

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + planned-catalog operation-mixing guard
evidence; no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell
editing, and no package format expansion.

Goal: prove the P8.363 materialize-after-replacement guard follows the current
planned worksheet name after a public replacement is renamed.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)`
  followed by `rename_sheet("Data", "QueuedData")`.
- The regression verifies pending replacement diagnostics migrate from `Data`
  to `QueuedData`, then test-hook materialization of planned `QueuedData` from
  source `Data` is rejected before private materialized-session mutation.
- The failure preserves public pending change count, replacement cell
  diagnostics, and `last_edit_error()` state. A different clean sheet can still
  be materialized through the test hook.
- Final `save_as()` verifies the workbook catalog keeps `QueuedData`, the
  queued replacement payload is written, old source sheet data is not restored,
  and the cleanly materialized different sheet remains byte-preserved.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not make materialization read from current staged replacement
  payloads after rename. The policy remains reject-first until public
  `WorksheetEditor` save/flush semantics are finalized.
- This does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, public `set_cell()` /
  `erase_cell()`, automatic public dirty-session flushing, relationship repair,
  sharedStrings/style migration, calcChain rebuild, formula evaluation, or
  XML/schema repair.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + planned-catalog operation-mixing guard
evidence; no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell

Goal: prove the materialize-after-replacement guard is independent of whether

  `replace_sheet_data("RenamedThenReplaced", ...)`.

  mutation.
- The failure preserves public pending change count, replacement cell
  diagnostics, and `last_edit_error()` state. A different clean sheet can still
  be materialized through the test hook.
- Final `save_as()` verifies the workbook catalog keeps the renamed sheet, the
  queued replacement payload is written, and old source sheet data is not
  restored.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not make materialization read from current staged replacement

  `WorksheetEditor` save/flush semantics are finalized.

## P8.366 - Flush materialized session after rejected public operations

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + operation-mixing flush hygiene; no public API,
no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no package
format expansion.

Goal: prove rejected same-sheet public operations do not poison a dirty private
materialized session, and that the explicit internal materialized flush still
uses the private session payload rather than any rejected public payload/name.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers a test-hook-only materialized `Data`
  session dirtied at `A1`, followed by rejected same-sheet
  `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)` and `rename_sheet("Data", "BlockedRename")`.
- The regression verifies both rejected public operations fail before queuing
  public changes, before staging replacement diagnostics, and before clearing
  dirty private state.
- A later explicit
  `testing_workbook_editor_flush_materialized_sessions_to_patch_plan()` queues
  exactly one internal worksheet projection and clears dirty state.
- Final `save_as()` verifies the output keeps `name="Data"`, writes the
  materialized cell payload, refreshes the sparse-store dimension, and does not
  leak the rejected replacement payload or rejected sheet name.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not make rejected public operations retryable transactions, does not
  make rejected replacement payloads materialization sources, and does not make
  dirty materialized sessions persist through public `save_as()` automatically.
- This does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, public `set_cell()` /
  `erase_cell()`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, calcChain
  rebuild, formula evaluation, or XML/schema repair.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.367 - Preserve flushed materialized projection after rejected public operations

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + post-flush operation-mixing hygiene; no public
API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no package
format expansion.

Goal: prove that once a dirty private materialized session has been explicitly
flushed into the Patch plan, later rejected same-sheet public operations do not
remove or overwrite that staged worksheet projection.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers materializing `Data`, dirtying `A1`,
  explicitly flushing the materialized projection, and then attempting
  same-sheet `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)` and
  `rename_sheet("Data", "BlockedAfterFlush")`.
- The regression verifies those later public operations remain reject-first
  while the clean private materialized session still exists.
- The staged projection count remains unchanged, replacement diagnostics remain
  empty, and the private session remains clean.
- Final `save_as()` verifies the output keeps the original sheet name and
  flushed materialized worksheet payload, while omitting the rejected
  replacement payload and rejected rename.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not define public materialized-handle invalidation, automatic dirty
  materialized-session persistence, public retry semantics, transaction
  semantics, or a public `WorksheetEditor` API.
- This does not add relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration,
  calcChain rebuild, formula evaluation, range metadata recalculation, or
  XML/schema repair.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

## P8.368 - Preserve materialized dirty state across cross-sheet public edit recovery

Status: done.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + cross-sheet operation-mixing hygiene; no
public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and no
package format expansion.

Goal: prove that after rejected same-sheet public operations on a dirty private
materialized session, a valid public edit on another sheet can still proceed
and clear the public error without clearing or replacing the dirty materialized
session.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers materializing and dirtying `Data`, then
  rejecting same-sheet `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)` and
  `rename_sheet("Data", "BlockedBeforeOtherEdit")`.
- A follow-up `replace_sheet_data("Untouched", ...)` succeeds, clears the prior
  public `last_edit_error()`, records replacement diagnostics only for
  `Untouched`, and leaves the dirty `Data` materialized session dirty.
- A later explicit materialized-session flush queues the `Data` projection
  alongside the public `Untouched` replacement and clears dirty private state.
- Final `save_as()` verifies `Data` contains the materialized payload,
  `Untouched` contains the cross-sheet public replacement, and rejected
  same-sheet payload/name data does not leak.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not make same-sheet public operations legal after materialization,
  does not add automatic dirty materialized-session persistence, and does not
  define public retry/transaction semantics.
- This does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, public `set_cell()` /
  `erase_cell()`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, calcChain
  rebuild, formula evaluation, range metadata recalculation, or XML/schema
  repair.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.workbook_editor" --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed.

Type: WorkbookEditor regression + cross-sheet catalog operation-mixing hygiene;
no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, and
no package format expansion.

Goal: prove the P8.368 recovery behavior also holds for a catalog-only public
edit on a different sheet: successful other-sheet rename clears the prior public
error without clearing dirty materialized state.

  planned sheet name, leaves replacement diagnostics empty, and keeps the `Data`

  alongside the other-sheet catalog rename and clears dirty private state.

  `OtherRenamedAfterRejectedOp`, preserves the renamed worksheet bytes, saves

Non-goals / boundary:
- This does not make same-sheet public operations legal after materialization,
  does not expose public materialized handles, and does not add automatic dirty

- This does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, public `set_cell()` /

## P8.370 - Review public API status before opening WorksheetEditor

Status: done.

Type: documentation / API gate refresh; no code, no public API, no public
header, no CMake membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: answer the current "what API still needs work?" question with a concrete
public/internal/future matrix, and keep the public `WorksheetEditor` header
closed until a deliberate implementation task opens it with tests.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now has a current
  public/internal/future API status matrix covering streaming, small new
  workbook, narrow `WorkbookEditor` Patch facade, internal materialized
  worksheet foundation, existing-file semantic objects, formula/calculation,
  and large worksheet rewrite.
- The same API document now records the post-P8.369 public `WorksheetEditor`
  preflight checklist: public-header gate, Doxygen non-goals, same-sheet
  operation-mixing rejection, cross-sheet recovery expectations, diagnostics
  separation, and required public tests before adding public symbols.
- `docs/NEXT_STEPS.md` and `docs/TASK_PLAN.md` now record P8.370 as a
  review-only API gate refresh rather than a public header opening.

Non-goals / boundary:
- Do not add `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `WorkbookEditor::try_worksheet()`, `get_cell()`,
  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` to public headers in this slice.
- Do not treat materialized-session test hooks as public random-cell editing.
- Do not claim sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair/pruning,
  range metadata recalculation, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild,
  XML/schema repair, or public `PackageEditor`.

Acceptance:
- At completion time the public header grep still found no public
  `WorksheetEditor` / `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols;
  this acceptance point is superseded by P8.378.
- Documentation clearly separates current public API, internal-only evidence,
  and future API targets.
- Since this is docs-only, validation is `git diff --check` plus trailing
  whitespace scan over the touched docs; CTest is not required.

## P8.371 - Preserve materialized move/save state with queued public edits

Status: done.

Type: internal `WorkbookEditor` materialized-session regression; no public API,
no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, no public header
expansion, no CMake membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: prove `WorkbookEditor` ownership transfer does not lose or cross-contaminate
future `WorksheetEditor` owner state when private materialized sessions coexist
with queued public Patch edits.

Output:
- Extended `test_internal_materialized_sessions_move_with_workbook_editor_impl()`
  in `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp`.
- The test now covers a dirty test-hook-only materialized `Data` session plus a
  queued public `replace_sheet_data()` for `Untouched` before move construction.
- Move construction preserves the dirty materialized session and queued public
  replacement diagnostics.
- Move assignment replaces the target editor's prior materialized session and
  queued public replacement instead of merging them.
- After assignment, the explicit internal materialized-session flush can stage
  the moved `Data` projection, and `save_as()` writes both the assigned source
  materialized payload and the assigned source cross-sheet public replacement.
- The output verifies discarded target-side materialized payloads and queued
  public replacement payloads do not leak into the assigned editor output.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This is still test-hook-only internal evidence. It does not expose
  `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`,
  `try_worksheet()`, public `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()`, public
  `PackageEditor`, or automatic dirty materialized-session persistence.
- This does not define public handle invalidation / reacquire semantics after
  move. The future public `WorksheetEditor` slice still needs public handle
  lifetime tests.
- This does not add sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair/pruning,
  range metadata recalculation, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, or XML
  schema repair.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes and covers move construction, move
  assignment, explicit flush after assignment, final save output, and
  discarded-target no-leak assertions.
- Public header grep still finds no public `WorksheetEditor` /
  `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for the touched test and
  docs.

## P8.372 - Preserve materialized state across save_as preflight failures

Status: done.

Type: internal `WorkbookEditor` materialized-session save-as failure regression;
no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, no
public header expansion, no CMake membership change, and no package format

  parent path while a test-hook-only materialized `Data` session is dirty but
  not yet flushed.
- The dirty session remains dirty, no staged projection is queued, and public
  `last_edit_error()` remains unchanged because save failures are not public

- After explicit internal materialized-session flush, the same test covers

  staged projection count and clean private session state are preserved.
- A later valid `save_as()` writes the materialized payload and refreshed

Non-goals / boundary:

- This does not add sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair/pruning,
  range metadata recalculation, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, or XML

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes and covers failed save before flush, failed
  save after flush, later valid save, and final output assertions.
- Public header grep still finds no public `WorksheetEditor` /
  `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for the touched test and
  docs.

## P8.373 - Reflush materialized edits after failed save_as

Status: done.

Type: internal `WorkbookEditor` materialized-session retry regression; no
public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, no
public header expansion, no CMake membership change, and no package format
expansion.

Goal: prove a failed `save_as()` after an explicit materialized-session flush
does not freeze or poison the private session, so a later edit can re-dirty,
reflush, and replace the earlier staged worksheet projection.

Output:
- Added
  `test_internal_materialized_session_reflush_after_failed_save_as()` in
  `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp`.
- The test materializes `Data`, writes a first payload, explicitly flushes it,
  and then triggers `save_as()` failure on an existing directory output path.
- The failed save preserves the clean private materialized session and the first
  staged projection diagnostic.
- The same session can be mutated again after the failed save, becomes dirty,
  and can be explicitly flushed again.
- Final `save_as()` writes the second payload and proves the first staged
  projection was replaced rather than leaked.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This is still test-hook-only internal retry evidence. It does not expose
  `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`,
  `try_worksheet()`, public `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()`, public
  `PackageEditor`, or automatic dirty materialized-session persistence.
- This does not define public automatic flush-on-save, handle transaction
  semantics, undo/history, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship
  repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range metadata
  recalculation, or XML schema repair.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes and covers first flush, failed save, retry
  mutation, reflush, final valid save, and earlier staged-payload absence.
- Public header grep still finds no public `WorksheetEditor` /
  `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for the touched test and
  docs.

## P8.374 - Reflush moved materialized edits after failed save_as

Status: done.

Type: internal `WorkbookEditor` materialized-session ownership/retry regression;
no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell editing, no
public header expansion, no CMake membership change, and no package format
expansion.

Goal: prove the private materialized-session retry path remains valid after
`WorkbookEditor` move construction / move assignment when assigned
materialized state coexists with queued cross-sheet public Patch edits.

Output:
- Added
  `test_internal_materialized_session_move_reflush_after_failed_save_as()` in
  `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp`.
- The test starts with a dirty test-hook-only materialized `Data` session and a
  queued public `replace_sheet_data()` for `Untouched`.
- Move construction and move assignment transfer the assigned source state and
  discard the target editor's prior materialized session and queued public
  replacement.
- After explicit materialized-session flush, `save_as()` to an existing
  directory fails without consuming the staged projection or queued public edit.
- The assigned materialized session can then be modified again, re-dirtied, and
  re-flushed.
- Final `save_as()` writes the later materialized projection, keeps the assigned
  cross-sheet public replacement, and verifies the earlier materialized payload
  plus discarded target-editor state do not leak.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This remains test-hook-only internal state-hygiene evidence. It does not
  expose `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `set_cell()` /
  `erase_cell()`, public `PackageEditor`, or automatic dirty
  materialized-session persistence.
- This does not define public `WorksheetEditor` move/reacquire semantics,
  public transaction semantics, undo/history, sharedStrings/style migration,
  relationship repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range
  metadata recalculation, or XML schema repair.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes and covers move construction, move
  assignment, explicit flush, failed save, retry mutation, reflush, final valid
  save, assigned public edit preservation, and discarded-target no-leak
  assertions.
- Public header grep still finds no public `WorksheetEditor` /
  `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for the touched test and
  docs.

## P8.375 - Reflush materialized edits after successful save_as

Status: done.

regression; no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public random-cell

  materialized payload, and saves it to a first output path.
- The same editor/session can be modified again after the successful save,
  becomes dirty, and can be explicitly re-flushed.
- A second `save_as()` writes the later materialized projection to a separate
  output path.
- The first output artifact still contains the first projection, and the second
  output contains the second projection without leaking the earlier staged
  payload.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This remains test-hook-only internal reuse evidence. It does not expose
  `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,

  relationship repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range
  metadata recalculation, or XML schema repair.

Acceptance:

  artifact separation.
- Public header grep still finds no public `WorksheetEditor` /
  `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for the touched test and
  docs.

## P8.377 - Clear target materialized state on assignment from moved-from source

Status: done.

Type: internal `WorkbookEditor` materialized-session moved-from assignment
cleanup regression; no public API, no public `WorksheetEditor`, no public
random-cell editing, no public header expansion, no CMake membership change,
and no package format expansion.

Goal: prove move assignment from an already moved-from `WorkbookEditor` source
does not leave stale target-side materialized or public edit state saveable.

Output:
- Added
  `test_internal_materialized_session_assignment_from_moved_from_source_clears_target()`
  in `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp`.
- The test moves an editor with a dirty private materialized `Data` session into
  a holder, making the source editor moved-from.
- A separate target editor starts with its own dirty private materialized
  `Untouched` session and queued public `replace_sheet_data()` for `Data`.
- Assigning from the moved-from source clears the target editor's materialized
  sessions, dirty materialized state, queued public edits, replacement
  diagnostics, and `last_edit_error()`, and makes `save_as()` throw rather than
  saving stale target state.
- The prior moved-to holder remains valid: it can explicitly flush and save its
  materialized payload without receiving the cleared target public or
  materialized payloads.

Non-goals / boundary:
- This remains test-hook-only internal cleanup evidence. It does not expose
  `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `set_cell()` /
  `erase_cell()`, public `PackageEditor`, or automatic dirty
  materialized-session persistence.
- This does not define public materialized handle invalidation, public move
  recovery semantics, transaction history, sharedStrings/style migration,
  relationship repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range
  metadata recalculation, or XML schema repair.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes and covers source moved-from setup,
  target-side materialized/public stale-state cleanup, target save rejection,
  holder preservation, holder flush/save, and no-leak assertions.
- Public header grep still finds no public `WorksheetEditor` /
  `WorksheetEditorOptions` / random-cell editing symbols.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for the touched test and
  docs.

## P8.378 - Open first public WorksheetEditor slice

Status: done.

Type: public API implementation, Doxygen update, public regression tests, and
task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format expansion.

Goal: open the narrow small-file In-memory random cell editing path under the
existing `WorkbookEditor` facade without turning Patch replacement or Streaming
writer APIs into hidden DOM/random-access paths.

Output:
- Added public `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet(name,
  options)`, and borrowed `WorksheetEditor` handle methods: `name()`,
  `try_cell(row, column)`, `set_cell(row, column, value)`,
  `erase_cell(row, column)`, `cell_count()`, and
  `estimated_memory_usage()`.
- `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` now performs output path preflight, then
  auto-flushes dirty materialized worksheet sessions into the internal Patch
  plan before writing.
- Public tests cover source cell reads, missing sparse cell reads,
  set-cell save-as roundtrip, erase-cell save-as roundtrip, max-cells
  materialization failure hygiene, same-sheet `replace_sheet_data()` /
  `rename_sheet()` rejection after materialization, and cross-sheet Patch
  coexistence.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`, add/delete worksheet, semantic
  sheet rename, non-default `StyleId` support, sharedStrings/style migration,
  relationship repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild,
  range/table/drawing metadata sync, or large-file low-memory random editing.
- `WorksheetEditor` is a borrowed handle into `WorkbookEditor`-owned state; it
  is not a standalone owner or public `PackageEditor` view.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public-header grep now finds the intended symbols
  (`WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`,
  `WorksheetEditor::try_cell()`, `set_cell()`, `erase_cell()`) and still does
  not find `try_worksheet()` or `get_cell()`.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.379 - Add try_worksheet and get_cell public slice

Status: done.

Type: public API implementation, Doxygen update, public regression tests, and
task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format expansion.

Goal: complete the next narrow `WorksheetEditor` convenience slice without
widening existing-workbook editing semantics into style/sharedStrings migration,
relationship repair, or large-file random access.

Output:
- Added `WorkbookEditor::try_worksheet(name, options)` returning
  `std::optional<WorksheetEditor>`. Missing current-planned sheet names return
  `std::nullopt`; option mismatch, queued same-sheet `replace_sheet_data()`,
  unsupported source worksheet payloads, and malformed source worksheet XML
  still throw `FastXlsxError`.
- Added `WorksheetEditor::get_cell(row, column)`. Missing sparse records throw
  `FastXlsxError`; explicit blank records return `CellValue::blank()`.
- Public tests cover missing-sheet optional lookup without diagnostics
  pollution, existing-sheet read/mutate/save, option mismatch and queued
  replacement mixing failures, missing-cell throw without store mutation, and
  explicit blank distinction.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No non-default `StyleId` support, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship
  repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range/table/drawing
  metadata sync, workbook-level in-memory guardrails, add/delete worksheet, or
  large-file low-memory random editing.
- `get_cell()` is a strict read helper, not a blank-default convenience API;
  callers expecting missing sparse cells should use `try_cell()`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public-header grep now finds the intended new symbols
  (`WorkbookEditor::try_worksheet()` and `WorksheetEditor::get_cell()`).
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.380 - Add WorksheetEditor A1 cell-reference overloads

Status: done.

Type: public API convenience implementation, Doxygen update, public regression
tests, and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format
expansion.

Goal: add a narrow single-cell A1 string convenience layer to the existing
small-file In-memory `WorksheetEditor` public slice while keeping row/column
overloads as the primary contract and without broadening existing-workbook
editing semantics.

Output:
- Added strict uppercase A1 overloads for `WorksheetEditor::try_cell()`,
  `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, and `erase_cell()`.
- The parser accepts only one cell reference such as `A1` or `XFD1048576`.
  Lowercase references, ranges, zero or leading-zero rows, zero columns, and
  coordinates outside Excel limits are rejected before calling the row/column
  overloads.
- Public tests cover A1 read/mutate/erase/save-as persistence, dimension refresh
  through the existing materialized-session handoff, invalid read references
  without store mutation or `last_edit_error()` updates, and invalid mutation
  references with no store mutation plus `last_edit_error()` updates.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No range parsing/iteration, sparse record iteration, row insertion/deletion,
  add/delete worksheet, non-default `StyleId` support, sharedStrings/style
  migration, relationship repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild,
  range/table/drawing metadata sync, workbook-level in-memory guardrails, or
  large-file low-memory random editing.
- A1 overloads are strict convenience wrappers over row/column semantics; they
  do not introduce a new coordinate model.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public-header grep finds the intended A1 overloads without adding range APIs.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.381 - Add WorksheetEditor sparse cell snapshot

Status: done.

Type: public API inspection convenience, Doxygen update, public regression
tests, and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format
expansion.

Goal: add a narrow owning snapshot API for the current materialized sparse
`WorksheetEditor` state so callers can inspect small worksheets without
borrowing internal `CellStore` iterators or changing dirty state.

Output:
- Added `WorksheetCellReference` and `WorksheetCellSnapshot` public value types.
- Added `WorksheetEditor::sparse_cells()`, returning an owning row-major
  `std::vector<WorksheetCellSnapshot>` copied from active sparse records.
- Public tests cover row-major order, source-backed values, edited cells,
  explicit blank records, erased source-cell omission, owning-copy behavior,
  no `last_edit_error()` update on snapshot reads, and save-as compatibility
  through the existing dirty materialized-session handoff.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No internal iterator exposure, borrowed lifetime handles, range filtering,
  streaming sparse iterator, row insertion/deletion, non-default `StyleId`
  support, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair/pruning, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range/table/drawing metadata sync,
  workbook-level in-memory guardrails, or large-file low-memory random editing.
- The snapshot copies `CellValue` payloads and coordinates only; it does not
  expose worksheet metadata, relationships, source XML offsets, or Patch
  `EditPlan` state.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public-header grep finds `WorksheetCellReference`, `WorksheetCellSnapshot`,
  and `WorksheetEditor::sparse_cells()` without adding range iterator APIs.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.382 - Add WorksheetEditor sparse range snapshot

Status: done.

Type: public API inspection convenience, Doxygen update, public regression
tests, and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format
expansion.

Goal: add a narrow range-filtered owning snapshot API for the current
materialized sparse `WorksheetEditor` state so callers can inspect active
records in a small rectangular range without borrowing internal `CellStore`
iterators or building a dense matrix.

Output:
- Added `WorksheetEditor::sparse_cells(CellRange)`, returning an owning
  row-major `std::vector<WorksheetCellSnapshot>` copied from active sparse
  records whose coordinates fall inside the 1-based inclusive range.
- The overload reuses existing `CellRange` / Excel limit validation, returns
  only records that currently exist in the materialized store, and does not
  synthesize missing cells as blank snapshots.
- Public tests cover source-backed values, explicit blank records, edited cells,
  range exclusion, empty missing-cell ranges, owning-copy behavior, invalid
  range rejection without store mutation or `last_edit_error()` updates, and
  save-as compatibility through the existing dirty materialized-session handoff.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No dense matrix read, synthetic missing-cell fill, internal iterator exposure,
  borrowed lifetime handles, streaming sparse iterator, row insertion/deletion,
  non-default `StyleId` support, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship
  repair/pruning, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, range/table/drawing
  metadata sync, workbook-level in-memory guardrails, or large-file low-memory
  random editing.
- The range snapshot copies `CellValue` payloads and coordinates only; it does
  not expose worksheet metadata, relationships, source XML offsets, or Patch
  `EditPlan` state.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public-header grep finds the intended `WorksheetEditor::sparse_cells(CellRange)`
  overload without adding dense range read or iterator APIs.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.383 - Add WorksheetEditor dirty state inspection

Status: done.

Type: public API inspection convenience, Doxygen update, public regression
tests, and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format
expansion.

Goal: expose a worksheet-local dirty-state probe for the current materialized
`WorksheetEditor` session so callers can tell whether this borrowed handle still
has sparse cell edits waiting for `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` auto-flush.

Output:
- Added `WorksheetEditor::has_pending_changes()`, returning the current
  materialized session dirty flag.
- Public tests cover freshly materialized clean state, read-only inspection not
  dirtying the session, failed mutation preserving clean state, missing-cell
  erase staying clean, existing-record erase becoming dirty, save-as path
  preflight failure preserving dirty state, successful save-as clearing dirty
  state, and a later mutation dirtying / saving again.
- The API does not update `WorkbookEditor::last_edit_error()` and does not add
  a pending Patch handoff count until `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` flushes the
  dirty session.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No flush/commit operation, dirty count, workbook-level unsaved-change model,
  internal `EditPlan` exposure, dense range read, sparse iterator, metadata
  recalculation, non-default `StyleId` support, sharedStrings/style migration,
  relationship repair/pruning, or large-file low-memory random editing.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public-header grep finds `WorksheetEditor::has_pending_changes()` without
  adding Patch-plan inspection APIs.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.384 - Invalidate WorksheetEditor handles after owner move

Status: done.

Type: public handle lifetime regression, small implementation guard, Doxygen /
README / task-doc sync; no new public API symbol and no package format change.

Goal: make the P8.313 borrowed-handle rule enforceable for the current by-value
`WorksheetEditor` implementation, including the move-assignment edge where an
old target-side handle might otherwise find a new same-name materialized session.

Output:
- Added a `WorkbookEditor` handle generation counter and stored it in each
  borrowed `WorksheetEditor` handle at creation.
- `WorkbookEditor` move construction invalidates handles borrowed from the
  moved-from editor; callers must reacquire from the moved-to editor.
- `WorkbookEditor` move assignment invalidates handles borrowed from both the
  move-assigned source and the overwritten target editor; callers must reacquire
  from the assigned-to editor.
- Public regressions cover old-handle failures, reacquired-handle reads /
  mutations, save-as roundtrip of assigned materialized state, and the
  same-sheet target-handle case that must not attach to the assigned source
  session.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No shared ownership, reference counting, detached worksheet model, automatic
  handle retargeting, thread-safety contract, commit / close semantics, or
  transaction model.
- `WorksheetEditor::name()` remains a local planned-name label; session access
  methods are the invalidation boundary.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public docs state that handles must be reacquired after owner move or
  move-assignment.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.385 - Add WorkbookEditor dirty materialized sheet diagnostics

Status: done.

Type: public API inspection convenience, Doxygen update, public regression
tests, and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format
expansion.

Goal: let callers inspect which materialized `WorksheetEditor` sessions are
dirty and waiting for `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` auto-flush even when they do
not retain every borrowed worksheet handle.

Output:
- Added `WorkbookEditor::pending_materialized_worksheet_names()`, returning
  planned sheet names for dirty materialized sessions in current planned catalog
  order.
- Clean materialized sessions are omitted; failed mutations do not add names;
  failed save-as path preflight preserves dirty names; successful `save_as()`
  clears names after auto-flush.
- Public regressions cover fresh / clean state, dirty one-sheet and multi-sheet
  diagnostics, `last_edit_error()` inspection invariance, move construction,
  move assignment replacement of target dirty names, and save-as output.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No explicit flush/commit API, dirty count, cell-count summary, dense snapshot,
  internal `EditPlan` exposure, dependency audit exposure, whole-`<sheetData>`
  replacement inclusion, transaction model, or large-file low-memory random
  editing.
- This diagnostic does not replace `WorksheetEditor::has_pending_changes()`; it
  is the workbook-level planned-name list for dirty materialized sessions only.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Public docs state that the method does not flush, increment
  `pending_change_count()`, expose internal Patch state, include replacement
  payloads, or update `last_edit_error()`.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.386 - Include dirty materialized sessions in pending worksheet summaries

Status: done.

Type: public API diagnostic refinement, Doxygen update, public regression tests,
and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format expansion.

Goal: let `WorkbookEditor::pending_worksheet_edits()` be the single coarse
worksheet-level summary surface for queued Patch edits and dirty materialized
`WorksheetEditor` sessions, without forcing callers to separately join
`pending_materialized_worksheet_names()`.

Output:
- Extended `WorkbookEditorWorksheetEditSummary` with `materialized_dirty`,
  `materialized_cell_count`, and `estimated_materialized_memory_usage`.
- `pending_worksheet_edits()` now includes dirty materialized sessions in source
  workbook sheet-catalog order, alongside queued rename / whole-`<sheetData>`
  replacements.
- Clean materialized sessions are omitted; replacement-only summaries are not
  marked materialized dirty; dirty materialized summaries coexist with
  cross-sheet replacements.
- Failed `save_as()` preserves dirty materialized summaries; successful
  `save_as()` clears dirty materialized summary state after auto-flush unless
  the same worksheet still has a queued rename or replacement.
- Public regressions cover clean session omission, dirty materialized summary
  fields, cross-sheet replacement coexistence, failed / successful save-as
  behavior, moved-from emptiness, and move construction / move assignment state
  transfer.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No explicit flush/commit API, dense snapshot, public `EditPlan`, dependency
  audit exposure, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair,
  transaction model, or large-file low-memory random editing.
- This does not change `pending_change_count()` semantics: dirty materialized
  sessions are still not counted as Patch handoffs until `save_as()` auto-flush
  queues their projections.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Public docs state that summary inspection does not flush, increment
  `pending_change_count()`, expose internal Patch state, or update
  `last_edit_error()`.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.387 - Add workbook-level dirty materialized aggregate diagnostics

Status: done.

Type: public API diagnostic refinement, Doxygen update, public regression tests,
and task-doc sync; no CMake membership change and no package format expansion.

Goal: let callers inspect aggregate dirty materialized `WorksheetEditor` sparse
cell count and memory estimate at workbook level without retaining every
borrowed worksheet handle or joining per-sheet summaries.

Output:
- Added `WorkbookEditor::pending_materialized_cell_count()` and
  `WorkbookEditor::estimated_pending_materialized_memory_usage()`.
- The aggregate includes only dirty materialized sessions waiting for
  `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` auto-flush. Clean materialized sessions and queued
  whole-`<sheetData>` replacement payloads are omitted.
- Cell count aggregation matches the sum of dirty sessions'
  `WorksheetEditor::cell_count()` values and includes explicit blank records.
  Memory aggregation matches the sum of dirty sessions'
  `WorksheetEditor::estimated_memory_usage()` values; it is not process RSS and
  excludes source package bytes, generated XML chunks, `PackageEditor` staging
  files, ZIP writer buffers, and save-time package assembly costs.
- Public regressions cover clean-session omission, failed mutation hygiene,
  single- and multi-session aggregation, replacement-only exclusion, failed
  `save_as()` preservation, successful `save_as()` clearing, moved-from
  emptiness, and move construction / move assignment state transfer.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No explicit flush/commit API, dense snapshot, public `EditPlan`, dependency
  audit exposure, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair,
  transaction model, or large-file low-memory random editing.
- This does not change `pending_change_count()` semantics and does not report
  materialized auto-flush handoffs as whole-`<sheetData>` replacement
  diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Public docs state that aggregate inspection does not flush, increment
  `pending_change_count()`, expose internal Patch state, include replacement
  payloads, or update `last_edit_error()`.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.388 - Pin WorksheetEditor handle lifetime across save_as

Status: done.

Type: public behavior regression, Doxygen/docs clarification, and task-doc sync;
no new public symbol, no CMake membership change, and no package format
expansion.

Goal: answer whether old `WorksheetEditor` handles should be deleted after
`WorkbookEditor::save_as()` and pin the safer public contract: they are
same-owner borrowed handles, not detachable worksheet owners, and `save_as()` is
not a handle invalidation boundary.

Output:
- Added public regression coverage proving a same-owner `WorksheetEditor` handle
  remains valid after failed output-path preflight and after successful
  `save_as()`.
- The same handle can read the materialized sparse store after save, become dirty
  again after a post-save mutation, and reflush on a later `save_as()`.
- A later output file receives the post-save edit while the earlier output file
  remains unchanged.
- Public docs now state that owner move construction / move assignment remains
  the invalidation boundary; callers only need to reacquire handles after owner
  transfer, not after ordinary `save_as()`.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No explicit handle close/delete API, no detached worksheet owner, no commit /
  transaction model, no automatic session pruning, no public `EditPlan`, and no
  large-file low-memory random editing.
- This does not change queued public edit diagnostics, replacement diagnostics,
  or the existing reusable-output `save_as()` contract.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Public docs clearly answer that old handles should not be deleted after
  `save_as()`; they stay valid while the owner is unchanged.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.389 - Materialize source sharedStrings as WorksheetEditor text

Status: done.

Type: public `WorksheetEditor` source-load hardening, internal `CellStore`
loader support, Doxygen/docs clarification, and public regression tests; no new
public symbol, no CMake membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: let `WorksheetEditor` materialize source workbook cells stored as
`t="s"` shared string indexes into `CellValue::text(...)`, while keeping save
output on the current inline-string sparse `CellStore` projection.

Output:
- `load_cell_store_from_workbook_sheet()` resolves the workbook-owned
  sharedStrings relationship and reads the existing `xl/sharedStrings.xml`.
- Source worksheet `t="s"` + `<v>index</v>` cells are materialized as text.
- Repeated indexes, XML entity decoding, relationship target normalization,
  simple rich-text flattening, and invalid-index failure hygiene are covered by
  public `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` regressions.
- `save_as()` still writes materialized text as inline strings and preserves the
  source sharedStrings part bytes.

Non-goals / boundary:
- Standalone worksheet XML/chunk loaders still reject `t="s"` because they have
  no workbook-level sharedStrings table context.
- No sharedStrings rebuild, writeback, index migration, rich-text preservation,
  style migration, relationship repair, metadata synchronization, or large-file
  low-memory random editing.
- Invalid, missing, empty, or out-of-range shared string indexes fail during
  materialization instead of becoming blank cells.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Public/internal docs distinguish read-only source materialization from
  sharedStrings migration or production sharedStrings editing.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched headers,
  source, tests, and docs.

## P8.390 - Harden source sharedStrings materialization failure matrix

Status: done.

Type: internal workbook-backed `CellStore` loader guardrail tests,
public/source-load boundary documentation, and task-plan clarification; no new
public symbol, no CMake membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: lock down the negative behavior around P8.389 source sharedStrings
materialization so the narrow read-only import cannot be mistaken for
sharedStrings migration, relationship repair, or tolerant XML repair.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` now covers workbook-backed `CellStore` loader
  failures for duplicate sharedStrings relationships, external targets,
  query/fragment-qualified targets, missing target parts, wrong content type,
  malformed `xl/sharedStrings.xml`, missing/empty/non-numeric indexes, and
  out-of-range indexes.
- These failures are asserted to happen without exposing a partial `CellStore`
  and without poisoning the reusable `PackageReader`.
- Existing standalone worksheet XML loader coverage still proves generic
  worksheet-only loading rejects `t="s"` because it has no workbook-level string
  table context.
- The positive workbook-backed materialization path remains covered by P8.389:
  source `t="s"` cells can become `CellValue::text(...)`, while save output
  still emits inline strings and preserves the source sharedStrings part bytes.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No sharedStrings writeback, rebuild, index migration, string-table repair,
  relationship repair, rich-text preservation, tolerant malformed-XML recovery,
  or large-file low-memory random editing.
- No new public diagnostics object; errors continue to surface as
  `FastXlsxError` diagnostics through the existing materialization path.
- No change to non-default source style id rejection or the current
  inline-string save projection.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Public/internal docs distinguish guarded read-only source materialization from
  production sharedStrings editing or migration.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched tests and
  docs.

## P8.391 - Pin public sharedStrings metadata materialization failure hygiene

Status: done.

Type: public `WorksheetEditor` source-load failure-state regression tests and
docs; no new public symbol, no CMake membership change, and no package format
expansion.

Goal: prove invalid source sharedStrings workbook metadata reaches the public
`WorkbookEditor::try_worksheet()` / `worksheet()` facade as materialization
failure without dirtying editor state, updating `last_edit_error()`, or leaving
partial materialized sessions.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` covers duplicate sharedStrings relationship,
  missing sharedStrings target part, wrong sharedStrings content type, and
  malformed `xl/sharedStrings.xml`.
- Both `try_worksheet("Data")` and `worksheet("Data")` throw for these
  non-missing-sheet materialization failures and preserve the sharedStrings
  diagnostic.
- Failures preserve planned/source sheet catalog inspection,
  `has_pending_changes()==false`, `pending_change_count()==0`, empty pending
  materialized diagnostics, and no `last_edit_error()`.
- The same editor remains usable after failure: a later `replace_sheet_data()`
  plus `save_as()` succeeds and writes replacement text.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No new public diagnostics value/object, no tolerant repair, no relationship
  repair, no sharedStrings rebuild/writeback/migration, no rich-text
  preservation, no in-place save, and no large-file random editing.
- The full low-level metadata/index negative matrix remains internal
  `fastxlsx.package_reader` coverage from P8.390; this slice only proves public
  facade state hygiene for representative invalid sharedStrings metadata
  failures.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Docs distinguish public facade failure hygiene from sharedStrings production
  editing or migration.
- `git diff --check` and trailing whitespace scan pass for touched tests and
  docs.

Non-goals / boundary:

## P8.393 - Pin public source style-id materialization failure hygiene

Status: done.

Type: public `WorksheetEditor` source-load failure-state regression tests and
docs; no new public symbol, no CMake membership change, and no package format
expansion.

Goal: prove source worksheet style attributes have a narrow, explicit boundary:
canonical non-zero numeric source style ids materialize as passthrough handles,
while malformed or qualified style attributes still reach the public
`WorkbookEditor::try_worksheet()` / `worksheet()` facade as clean
materialization failures without becoming partial sessions or blocking later
Patch edits.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers a non-default source style id generated
  by the writer and proves dirty projection writes the same numeric `s` id back
  while preserving `xl/styles.xml`. This section originally also pinned an
  explicit default `s="0"` source style attribute as a failure; P8.466
  supersedes that narrow default-style boundary by normalizing source `s="0"`
  to no style handle, and later P8.467/P8.471 keep the malformed-token boundary
  exact.
- For malformed source style ids, both `try_worksheet("Data")` and
  `worksheet("Data")` throw with the current invalid style id diagnostic.
- Failures preserve source/planned catalog inspection, leave no pending edits
  or dirty materialized sessions, and do not update `last_edit_error()`.
- The same editor remains usable after failure: a later `replace_sheet_data()`
  plus `save_as()` succeeds and writes replacement text.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No source style preservation, style id validation, style migration, style
  merge, stylesheet semantic editing, relationship repair, new diagnostics
  object, or large-file random editing.
- This does not change whole-`<sheetData>` Patch behavior, where caller-supplied
  `CellValue` style ids are still written as-is by the existing Patch facade.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Docs continue to distinguish non-default source style id rejection from
  future existing-workbook style migration or merge. Current docs must also
  mention the P8.466 `s="0"` default-style normalization exception and the
  P8.467/P8.471 exact-value-only boundary.
- `git diff --check` passes.

## P8.395 - Factor public materialization failure hygiene checks

Status: done.

Type: public `WorksheetEditor` test refactor, malformed source worksheet XML
facade regression, and docs; no behavior change, no new public symbol, no CMake
membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: keep the growing public materialization-failure matrix maintainable while
adding one XML-malformed public facade smoke.

Output:
- `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp` now uses a shared helper for the common
  `try_worksheet()` / `worksheet()` materialization-failure hygiene assertions:
  diagnostic propagation, no pending edits, no dirty materialized sessions, no
  `last_edit_error()` update, and a follow-up Patch save proving the editor is
  still usable.
- The existing public failure suites for invalid sharedStrings metadata, source
  style ids, unsupported source cell types, and invalid boolean payloads now
  reuse that helper instead of duplicating the same state checks.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now also covers malformed source worksheet XML
  with a missing closing worksheet root. `try_worksheet("Data")` and
  `worksheet("Data")` throw with the worksheet event reader diagnostic and do
  not dirty materialized state.
- Because the same malformed target worksheet also blocks same-sheet
  `replace_sheet_data()` preflight, the recovery assertion intentionally edits
  a different valid sheet to prove the editor session is not poisoned.

Non-goals / boundary:
- No XML repair, namespace repair, tolerant source worksheet recovery,
  same-sheet Patch bypass for malformed source worksheet XML, public
  diagnostics object, relationship repair, or large-file random editing.
- This does not change the runtime materializer or Patch helper behavior; it
  only factors tests and pins the public facade boundary.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- Full default CTest passes.
- Docs describe malformed target worksheet XML as blocking same-sheet Patch
  preflight while preserving editor recovery for unrelated valid sheets.
- `git diff --check` passes.
